Get the word out, and put on your best performance tonight. Do not give Manfred anything he can use against us.
And I want the ten best fighters to come with me.
As for you, I want you to tail Manfred... You saw where he was heading, right?
Yes, Captain.
Good.
Tonight, I'll rescue our 'spy.'
I've stayed here in Londinium for so long, I have to at least get one thing done before I can go home with my mind at ease, right?
I... I won't let our leader down again.
Horn, you're back!
I am. Where's Robben?
The team got back a while ago.
Good. Did they find any medicine? Let me check on Salley.
There's... There's no need.
What are you talking about?! Do you mean Salley...?
I still have to see him.
Victoria has lost another fine soldier.
A little girl has lost her father.
How many more must we lose before we finally see the end of this tragedy?
What was Salley's girl's name again? Was it Erin? Elisa? If memory serves, his family lived in County Ascarat.
Blake, pack up his belongings, please. Once the war is over, we'll pay his family a visit.
But... he didn't leave anything behind.
Nothing at all?
No, Blake... At the very least, he left a story. We have to go on this journey still. His daughter will want to hear his story.
I will tell her that, even in our darkest hour, her father never gave in to suffering.
Right. I'll come with you, Horn. Once the war is over.
Also, I have a message for you. From Salley.
What... did he say?
He said—'Sorry, Lieutenant, but you must live on.'
He apologized to me.
He did. And, I understood him. We all did.
As soldiers, we all considered that we could die out there somewhere, one day.
But after we saw all that, Salley... No, all of us understood what's far scarier.
Take a look at the state of Londinium. It's our home, but it's become a prison.
Is dying an honorable death too much to ask for?
It shouldn't be.
Indeed, but that's the truth now.
We all know that well, but... we... we want you to keep marching down this road, because you gave us hope.
Horn... You didn't have to carry all our hopes on your back. It's too much for one person.
It's harder... to live, huh?
Ha... Looks like I owe her an apology.
Who's 'her?'
A fine Victorian soldier, just like the rest of you.
She... Let's not talk about this now.
Tell everyone we don't have time to mourn.
Did you find out where they're keeping our people?
There's a high chance.
If the information I got turns out to be correct, the Sarkaz will move them tomorrow morning.
I... I was going to ask what you think.
You don't have to ask. Just give us the order!
Very well, then. We will begin as soon as the sun sets.
I've marked the most probable locations on the map. Any thoughts?
There are... eight spots?
Even with our operators, it'll be hard to check all these places in two days.
Unfortunately, all eight fulfill the requirements of a temporary prison.
There are no settlements near any of them, which means that it's difficult for news to get in and out.
They're all located near the highway that leads to Central Londinium, which facilitates the Sarkaz fighters' movement around the city.
And... none of them are connected to the plate we are in.
Did the Sarkaz commander figure out you're operating underground?
He's a smart man. We mustn't underestimate him.
Then... If this is where they're keeping prisoners, shouldn't there be a massive security detail guarding the place?
We thought about that too.
Unfortunately, even though our recon team managed to track the Sarkaz's movement in each plate, they haven't found any openings.

opts: What is this place?

lines.x: An abandoned factory. They say it used to make candy... And don't get the wrong idea. That is just what the civilians in the industrial districts usually call it.
What they actually refined is an organic compound called Loxic Kohl. It's packaged like sweets for children, so everyone's come to call it 'candy'.
A candy factory? Sounds familiar...

opts: Make this our top priority in the search.
I think we're pretty close to the answer.

lines.x: Dr. Doctor, may I know why?

opts: We may know who owns it.

lines.x: Ah, of course. You mean Mr. Thomas?
Mr. Thomas has been working with the Sarkaz, right? He said he saw the Sarkaz using his factory for some shady business, and he was almost silenced because of it.
Not to mention... he has some information that Dublinn wanted too.

opts: All the factions opposing the Sarkaz want it...
It's something the Sarkaz don't want anyone to know.

lines.x: Alright then.
Tell Feist that this factory is our prime objective.
Harmo, keep working on the move. We will keep that option open to us too.
Commander!
What is it, Lawrence?
The recon team is back. They ran into some Sarkaz mercenaries aboveground.
Casualties...?
Four injured, and one... captured.
Sorry everyone, the meeting ends here. I need to take a look.
Commander...
Rockrock, what are you doing? We just got your wounds bandaged!
No... I have something I must tell the commander.
Did you find her...?
Yes, we got news from Lady Golding. She's still at the school in the northern residential district, and she's safe...
But they were ambushed by the Sarkaz during a social gathering two days ago.
And the Messenger lady... was taken prisoner.
Wait a moment, please—
Is the Messenger you are referring to the one who requested you to rendezvous with Rhodes Island?

opts: The lady who helped us get into Victoria.
She gathered lots of information on Victoria.
Kal'tsit's old friend.

lines.x: Right... That's her.
Amiya, Dr.Doctor, our rescue operation has been moved up.
She's very important to the Self-Salvation Corps. She holds a lot of information, and this might even affect our evacuation operation.
Yes, I understand.
She's irreplaceable to Rhodes Island too.

opts: This will be our first joint operation.

lines.x: Good. I have faith that Rhodes Island will help us rescue our imprisoned Messenger from the Sarkaz—
Our strongest warrior, our most important ally, and our most trusted friend, Lady Heidi Thomson.
7:17 P.M. \ Overcast
Abandoned Liquor Plant, Sudean Borough, Londinium
Get moving! Don't just stand there! Get a move on!
The general said we gotta be done with these materials tonight and get them sent to the station!
Ungh...
What are you crying for? You slacking off?!
Ahh... P-Please don't hit me... I haven't slept for three or four days now... I'm really tired... Agh!
Stop it.
Who are you, and what gives you the right to stop me?!
Lady Heidi...
Don't worry, just get behind me.
I see. You're the new girl who just got here a couple days ago. You're... a teacher? A journalist?
Whatever, don't remember exactly, but you're one of those good-for-nothing scumbags. A dime a dozen in Victoria.
Save the smooth talk for the rich and noble. It doesn't work on us Sarkaz!
*cough* *cough*... *cough*.
Y-You're bleeding!
If you want to kill me and my friends, then do it, but don't forget that there are some hundred pairs of eyes staring at you right now.
What are you all looking at?!
They want to see how you're going to deal with me.
You think we'd be scared of a bunch of prisoners?
Of course not, but work waits for no one. Isn't that right? You said it yourself. Your general wants these materials done tonight.
Heh, maybe seeing how you two is about to end up will motivate them some.
If they figure out what awaits them tomorrow morning, do you think they'll still willingly spend their last night on Terra forging weapons for their enemies?
You...! How'd you know that tomorrow...
I also happen to know why you must let us live through the night.
Hey, she's taunting you. Don't be a moron and spill all the beans. Let's get going. We have work to do too.
I don't take orders from you!
That change of clothes really helped your temper, huh? Remember who was crying for his mother when he was fighting the Victorian remnants not all that long ago.
Piss off, you loudmouthed merc. Your head's gonna roll one day because of that tongue of yours.
You'll have that jerk who's been yelling about everything in my ears to thank. Trust me, I didn't use to talk like this.
I'm too busy to be wasting time on you morons. Fine, I'll let these people live a couple hours longer.
All of you... and you in particular. No funny moves.
Listen up! The Sarkaz got no tolerance for slackers! Anyone who's not working wins a date with our blades! Got it?!
Now get to work, if you know what's good for you!
We're safe for the time being.
I-I'm sorry, Lady Heidi. You got hurt because of me...
I'm fine. Just got my dress dirty.
You're always so calm and graceful... It puts my mind at ease just knowing you're here with us.
Then trust me once more, alright?
Go back inside and stay with your friends. The Sarkaz still need you to do their work. They won't hurt us, for the time being.
As for these mercenaries...
We'd better stop watching. We got work to do tonight, right?
But...
Come on, no buts. Hoederer told us to come and do whatever the soldiers say.
Okay, stop staring at the wall. This place is the farthest from the entrance. Not even a bug could fly in here. Look here—
—See how sturdy it is? Let's go.
7:27 P.M. \ Overcast
Underground structure, Sudean Borough, Londinium
Forward! Forward!
Report, Commander! The 3rd is all ready! Confirming Exit No. 9 clear—
Confirmed! We can exit safely!
Rockrock, what are you doing here? You're still wounded...
Captain, permission to rejoin the team?
But the commander said...
They have Bill. He covered me... and I can't just lie down here when I don't even know if he's still alive.
I will rescue him, Rockrock. I promise.
You might think I'm not a suitable leader...
If he was here, he would say that you are.
He always believed in you. We all believe in you.
You do...?
But this mission isn't like the covert rescue operations before.
We might run into a bunch of Sarkaz warriors head on and find ourselves in unprecedented danger.
Rockrock, before you joined the Self-Salvation Corps, you were just the daughter of a train stoker who'd only graduated secondary school.
You're just a mechanic yourself.
Haha, that's true.
The Sarkaz call us rebels... Does that really make us an army, though? We're just normal Londiniers.
You're absolutely right. I'm very normal.
I never remember all the righteous talk the commander preaches. The only thing on my mind is how to keep my family alive.
Feist, I'm actually really afraid of the Sarkaz...
But I'm more afraid of you dying somewhere when I'm not looking!
I know, Rockrock... I'm afraid too. I'm afraid of dying, and I'm even more afraid I could get the rest of you killed.
Phew... Haha.
It feels much better getting it off your chest, doesn't it?
Now, let's go.
Doctor.
Indra and I will guard this place with the Self-Salvation Corps. Dagda and Morgan are rendezvousing with Amiya.

opts: It's not often you split up.
Did something happen?

lines.x: There's no hiding anything from you, is there?
It's nothing important. There are just certain things that we have to deal with sooner or later, and we needed a suitable time for them.

opts: It's hard to push forward with expectations on your shoulders.
Need me to say anything to them?

lines.x: Are you all ready?
You've just arrived at the exit closest to the objective. From here to the factory, we'll have to cross two underground blocks.
Our squads are in position. They will make sure our trip to the objective goes smoothly.
I will leave you in command for the rest, Dr.Doctor.
Clovisia is a capable leader, and everyone has expectations of her and Amiya.
Doctor, There's been a lot on my mind throughout this journey, but there is still one thing that I haven't entirely thought through.
If there is one thing I have to take care of myself, this is it. If I can't even keep my closest friends in check, then what's the point of coming back here?
Doctor, we got a transmission from Feist. They've made it aboveground already. We need to rendezvous with them right away—

opts: It's time for us to leave.
Let's enjoy the Londinium air.

lines.x: Right, we'll be ready.
Haha... I would like to know, too.
I wish you... a safe journey.
7:55 P.M. \ Overcast
Outside the Eastern Entrance, Abandoned Liquor Plant, Sudean Borough, Londinium
Blake, give me a report of the situation up ahead.
The path is clear, over.
Robben?
Sniper team is in position.
Good. How many hostiles?
I got twelve in sight. All of them Sarkaz.
What about their commander? Do you see him?
I don't.
Stay frosty. Wait for my signal.
8:09 P.M. \ Overcast
Abandoned Liquor Plant, Sudean Borough, Londinium
Aaah... ah...
Ha... Haah... ah...
Benny, who's in that room?
Uh... the Self-Salvation Corps soldier they captured this afternoon.
Are they interrogating him?
The Sarkaz won't let the chance to get some Self-Salvation Corps intel slip away. That soldier... probably doesn't have much longer to live.
I hope he'll hang in there for a little longer. It won't be much longer now...
Are they here...?
No signs of anyone yet.
Then stay on the lookout.
How's Hoederer's search going?
The mercenaries are watching our prisoners in the cells. They've been working all day, but we have too many prisoners. It'll take time to check each one of them.
What's more, this guy we have in here is a stubborn one. We haven't managed to beat anything out of him...
Hm... Make good use of what time we have left, then. If he still doesn't give us anything...
Is it my time to shine, then?
Ugh—You, you...!
Put your blade down.
General! How did he...?!
Wait outside. I will take care of this.
Yes... Please be careful!
I'm sure you know that even if he actually tried to attack us, that blade wouldn't have done him much good.
Please... I would rather you stop startling my men. They're all fairly anxious as is.
Very well. We just didn't want to be late. Especially considering the guy inside isn't even breathing anymore.
You came at just the right moment, then.
Who's in charge of the south side of the factory?
Schwab's squad, Captain.
Schwab, huh...? He was in my squad five years ago.
Is that what you meant when you said I should pay attention to the shadows beneath my feet?
Get ahold of Schwab's position. No, have him come see me right now.
Benny, it's a little quiet outside the building, isn't it? Where are the mercenaries guarding the place? Are they gone?
Right, not one of them's still here.
They are acting quite strange...
They were strange to start with, no? The Sarkaz never made any sense, whether making friends or killing.
Don't say that unless you're trying to anger them.
Sorry, Lady Heidi... but I've been locked in here for two weeks. It's hard for me not to hate the Sarkaz.
No one blames you. You're doing very well.
Right, I heard the Sarkaz general paid us a visit at noon, with the mercenary leader.
Manfred did...?
Manfred... that's not good.
What's the matter?
This is a trap. Clovisia... and maybe them...

Are you worried that the commander will start the operation tonight? We need to find a way to get in touch with them... Uh... but it's been so many days, and we still haven't found a way to get any news past these walls.
No, I'm sure they've considered that this could be a trap. This might perhaps... be an opportunity too.
Benny, let everyone know that our operation might need to be pushed up. Anyone who can fight should get ready to fight.
But even if we can make it out of the factory building, it won't be easy to push through all the defenses outside the gate...
Then let's come this way.
A mercenary told me this wall here... is very safe.
8:45 P.M. \ Overcast
Outside the South Wall of the Abandoned Liquor Plant, Sudean Borough, Londinium
Higher... A little higher... Yep, this is a nice view, stable and all.
Closure, what do you see?
Hm... Three entrances. Each of 'em have some thirty men standing guard.
The ones guarding the entrance to the east are all Sarkaz. So are the ones to the southwest, actually... Uh, different outfits, though. Looks like they might be mercs.
And there's the main entrance to the north... It's the Dublinn forces who're guarding that one.
Then there are also some five or six squads patrolling the campus, going about between each factory building...
Huh? Wait up... The south... like, the wall closest to us... This place's empty.
Empty?
Going by how things are laid out everywhere else, there should be a squad patrolling here, too... but there doesn't seem to be one.

opts: Might be a trap.
Perhaps we have a helping hand.

lines.x: Doctor, should we proceed as planned?

opts: What do you think?

lines.x: We need to rescue Lady Heidi, and we also need to get to Central Londinium as soon as possible... No matter the case, we don't have any time to waste.

opts: What about the Self-Salvation Corps?

lines.x: We'll do as you say in this operation. Commander's orders.
We have someone to rescue too.
Doctor, I was going to say that no matter what your call is, we've got our operation... but my brothers in the 3rd are right. You're the commander.

opts: Alright, we'll proceed as planned.
Team Morgan, to the east entrance.
The 3rd will split up and distract the north and southwest entrances.
Closure will stay on surveillance and support.

lines.x: Alright, Doctor. Dagda, let's go.
Roger that. Let's move!
No problem. Leave it to me.

opts: Amiya, send a signal to the others.

lines.x: Others? Are there other Rhodes Islanders aboveground?
Right, Mr. Feist. As soon as they get our signal, they'll know that we'll have an operation here tonight.
Do you see them, Doctor? I'm guessing not yet, right? They aren't close enough.
You said they aren't nearby? Uh, does the Doctor have some kind of special Arts?

opts: No, PRTS is helping me.
I've got X-ray vision.

lines.x: What... That sounds like some amazing technology.
Doctor, you have to tell me more about it once we get back.
Doctor, you aren't pulling my leg, are you?
You have to have some kind of special terminal. The kinds with displays and all!
But if someone is here, should we wait outside?
No need. If they determine there's a need to rendezvous with us, it'll be after we head inside.
Then the problem at hand is still how we get inside...
Doctor, it's your call. Which way?

opts: Let's go south.

lines.x: That's a wall...
Ah! I get it.
Closure, Mr. Feist, do you have any way for us to climb over there?
We could blow it up? We made a buncha new demolition devices. Couldn't have blown Londinium's outer walls, but they could work here.
Uh... it might be difficult.
Londinium's factories are usually built with reinforced outer walls. Unless you blow it up from the inside, it probably won't have as much of an effect as you'd expect.
Then... How about if I pack in some more firepower?
No, that'll attract too much attention. Morgan's efforts will go to waste.
Alright... I guess we should've had Morgan take some with her.
How about we use grappling hooks? Rockrock and I use them sometimes to hide from the patrols.
Johnny, bring the stuff over here.
You got it, Captain. I almost thought I was carrying it for nothing.
Preparedness averts peril.
I need Rock-5 to mark the position...
Oh, right, I almost forgot. Here!
Is this... Rock-17? Why do you have it?
Because I was working on it the whole time on our way up! Look, it's all done. It's got Closure's Seal of Quality on it!
Uh... I'm not sure my Seal of Quality is any good to you, though...
I checked it out. It's running well.
What's this ear?
I added it while I was at it! All Closure brand drones get one!
Crap... You don't like it? If you don't like it...
No, no need to fix it.
Thanks.
Nothing wrong with the drone, then? I'll take the Doctor, Johnny, and Gabby with me. Rockrock... Can you take Amiya with you?
Alright.
I'll launch the device, then—
Rock-17, aim!
Anchor secured!
Let's do this, Miss Rockrock.
Hold tight, Doctor. The wall isn't that tall, but they call these things ziplines for a reason.

opts: So I'm not getting out of this, huh?
When do I get to be master of my own fate?

lines.x: Alright... Are we all ready?
This might be our only chance—
3, 2, 1—Launch!
Enemy sighted! She's got steel claws—
That's one.
You took a Sarkaz down with just one hit? Not bad, Feline. You in the Victorian Army?
Stuff it!
You're strong, not like those Dublinn weaklings. Very well, Feline, you're worthy of dying by my blade!
—Watch out!
Let's not jump to conclusions about who's dying by whose blade just yet.
Ahh—
Sarkaz, you can't imagine how much I want to end your life right now... Too bad my sword is broken, thanks to your friend there.
Dagda, snap out of it!
I know what you're thinking. You want revenge, right? This isn't the time. We promised the Doctor we'd make a ruckus, draw the enemies here, and then leave—
Heh... Feline, you've killed a Sarkaz or two before! Your life... a Sarkaz will claim your life one day!
I don't care about my life all that much.
If it was up to me, I wouldn't have run away from you people.
What's gotten into you? You aren't even dodging their Arts!
I can take a few more Sarkaz down before I die...
I shouldn't have brought you here... Don't forget, Vina's still waiting for you down below!
Siege...
If you want to die, suit yourself, but don't get the Doctor and Amiya killed! You'd bring shame to Vina's name!
Fine. Morgan, tell me. How much longer do we need to hold them?
Five minutes. Then the Doctor said someone would be here to pick us up.
There's a fight up ahead.
Right, don't know who they are, but looks like they beat us to the bunch, fighting the Sarkaz.
Blake, do you remember how I said I got away from Dublinn?
You said someone helped you...
Right. I didn't see his face, but I knew he's strong. So strong that he could walk into their campground alone even though there were hundreds of them.
Oh? If he was that strong, why'd he need such a ruckus?
It's not necessarily him, but he definitely isn't alone. There's a team who've been fighting Dublinn and the Sarkaz in Sudean.
Blake, I've never been so sure—
We aren't the only resistance members left. We... still have friends out there, spreading fire under the night sky.
Should we go help them, then?
No, we can't change our objective just yet.
Let's move up and sneak inside while our friends have the Sarkaz distracted.
Horn! There's a Dublinn team closing in on you. Watch out! Their leader is... is...
Mandragora.
Soldier.
A fire...?
Right. They reported to me they heard fighting just now.
Hm. Where are the guards?
Most of them are still here. They haven't been drawn away yet.
I knew it... They came prepared.
Should we proceed, then?
Let's watch the fire outside for the time being. Our operation has begun.
Have everyone get ready.
Remember. This is our only chance.
Hey you! What's this? Did you break the factory's machinery?!
Who let you in here... It's you?!
Hello there.
Heh, I knew you were planning something. Get over here!
Whatever I, of all people, might be planning shouldn't mean anything to a warrior like you.
Ever since the Steam Knights vanished without a trace, and the Knights of the Tower fell one after another, how many warriors are there in Londinium... no, all of Victoria, worthy of your attention?
What's with the flattery all of a sudden, Feline? Trying to get me to let my guard down? You underestimate the Sarkaz.
You're right. I can't kill you tonight. Not yet. But that's not going to stop me from sticking this arrowhead against your throat.
Ngh...
A pampered, high-class lady like you is going to bleed with just a little scratch to your throat, I'm sure.
Haha, I'll let you stand here and watch your friends die one by one. By next morning, you'll've bled completely dry too—
If... If my blood will satisfy you...
Take this!
Ahhhh! W-What is this?! My eyes! It burns!
Ugh––!
Lady, are you alright?!
It's just a scratch. It'll stop bleeding soon.
Did you see that? The Sarkaz fell for it! The reagent you told me to keep actually worked!
Let's have him lay down here for a little. Just taking his crossbow and knocking him out almost broke my hand...
The Sarkaz are a hardy bunch. If their eyes weren't always full of blood and hatred, he would've noticed you sneaking up to him.
Never mind... Benny, how is everyone else doing?
We did what you said and took over the factory. We probably won't be able to hold it for too long, though. The Sarkaz are on their way, and they won't fall for this twice.
We'd better hurry, then.
General, they're here.
Which entrance?
All three are under attack.
I'm not surprised.
What about the Dublinn forces?
They're fighting the rebels.
Where's Mandragora?
We haven't seen their leader.
I see. Perhaps she'd be interested in what is about to take place here.
What about Hoederer?
Er...
I'm here.
I thought you were meant to guard the southwest entrance?
Before I head back to the fight, there is one thing I have to tell you.
*cough*... hah...
An injured mercenary... Who is he?
His name is Schwab. We had some dealings with each other, but he betrayed me, leading to some unexpected hiccups.
Betrayed...? That's an awfully terrible way to put it. Hoederer... Have you forgotten who you are?
Maybe you're the one who's forgotten himself, Schwab.
We're mercenaries... Hah... we've never been... anything else.
Hoederer, she's here... I haven't told her about you... Now don't you see what a great friend I am? Haha... I really can't wait to see... the look on your faces...
I have no idea what you are talking about.
I have no need to listen to a traitor talk.
Manfred—
Hoederer, you needn't explain anything.
All I need to know is if you still serve me.
No question there.
Good, then. I have a new mission for you.
Rockrock, Johnny, Gabby... All of you landed? Good.
Phew... We've made it in. Doctor, are you doing okay?

opts: I made it through. Somehow.
I'm about used to this.

lines.x: Sorry, I haven't ever had a passenger, and all I could think about was what if the grappling hook couldn't support the two of us.
That's why I had to hold you so tight.

opts: At least we landed safely.
I thought you'd be more dependable.

lines.x: Closure, can you see what's going on inside?
I can see everything no problem, Amiya. Even the fur in your ears...
*Cough*, why is your drone so close?! I-It's too loud!
Besides... Why can I hear you speaking through it? Why aren't we using the comms?
The comms could be bugged! I mean, you said Sarkaz commander's a real smart cookie!
Not my Cloclo-1651 though. It's running an all-new security system. That's right. The one I was installing on the whole ship before we left...
Cloclo-1651?
Uh... Rockrock...? You don't mind, right? I kind of took some inspiration from your drones...
It's okay. I like the name... It's cute.
Awesome!
Cloclo-... uh, some number... start scanning! Our target—Heidi Thomson!
Target... The target's not far off. The target... Crap! Sarkaz troops! Enemies closing in!
Everyone, be careful. Get ready to fight!
What are you people doing here?
Watch out!
Are you... rebels? Like a finfish caught in a net... Haha!
Miss Amiya, there are a dozen Sarkaz behind him!
Rock-17—
Let me do it.
Black lines—You're the Cautus!
Warrior.
I will ask you this. Are you willing to surrender?
Surrender...? Cautus, what kind of joke is that?! Just watching you wield this power makes me sick to my stomach!
I'll never let you touch my emotions!
You've seen Arts like this before?
Don't make me laugh! All the Sarkaz here have seen it before!
I'd like to kill you myself... but before that, I have to tell the general...
Uh, someone attacked him from behind? A crossbow bolt... Where from?
Rock-17, aim!
Hello, my Self-Salvation Corps friends, and also... my Rhodes Island friends whom I haven't met.
Good evening, everyone.
Lady... Heidi?
That's right. We meet at last, Miss Amiya.
I don't have time to explain. Doctor... You're the Doctor, right? You're exactly as Kal'tsit described.

opts: Is that a compliment?
A nice description, I hope.

lines.x: Yes... even your reaction is about what I expected. Doctor, have you thought through how we'll make it out of here?
Over here! They're here! Come—
Urghh—
Quiet down, Sarkaz. I won't let you draw the other fighters here that quickly.
Thanks, Benny. Where are the others?
Milady, we've taken the factory on the south side.
The methods you taught us are working really well... Even the frailest weavers are managing to fight off the Sarkaz guards.
Everyone's using the tools and machines they're familiar with, after all.
These factories are ours, Benny. The Sarkaz can't control everything here as easily as they thought.
Lady Heidi... I never expected you to be such a formidable fighter.
Miss Amiya...
Please, call me Amiya. Just like Dr. Kal'tsit does.
Okay, Amiya.
Kal'tsit taught me a lot of things...
And yes, that includes fighting.
Too bad I wasn't her best student.
I was never able to help her with much.
But I will do my absolute best to help Rhodes Island in this battle, just as I fought alongside the Self-Salvation Corps.
Another team of Sarkaz soldiers is coming our way.
Right, I don't want to be a killjoy... but things are pretty tense right now. There's not just one team... Uh, it looks like all the Sarkaz fighters in the factory are coming our way.

opts: They know they lost the building.

lines.x: Right, Doctor. We did our best, but this bought us very little time.
That's why I need you to think of a way to get out of this right now...

opts: A friend of ours should be here soon.
He's waiting on the east side already.

lines.x: A friend...?
And 'he...?'
Is it just a single man? Or a whole team?
Doctor, the Sarkaz soldiers that Manfred trained are very hard to deal with. They could be hiding anywhere... We mustn't let our guard down.
Everyone, I... I'm seeing something really strange! It's way too weird! The Sarkaz up ahead are fighting... Uh, I mean, they're fighting other Sarkaz.
Wait, are those... Sarkaz mercs?
It's the strange mercenaries... One of them seemed to have lent us a hand this afternoon, too.
Sarkaz mercenaries?
No, we can't trust them! They're the ones who took Bill this afternoon... *cough*! I...
Rockrock, calm down. You're still wounded.
I'm fine, but I can't say that about Bill!
Captain, help me out... They need to understand.
Lady Heidi, you said the Sarkaz mercenaries helped you. Could you be more specific about that?
It was like they were sending me a signal...
But I wasn't entirely sure...
What kind of signal?
All these Victorian soldiers... Should we kill them?
Bloody Dublinn! I'll...
Captain, why aren't you attacking?
Horn! Why are you stopping me? Have you forgotten how many of our brothers and sisters died at their hands?
Before she casts her Originium Arts, the Dublinn Caster always raises her hands.
It was those hands and that staff that took Cello's life... as well as the lives of many other Victorian soldiers.
Horn puts her hand on the crossbow she hid up in her sleeve.
If Mandragora shows any signs of lifting even a single finger, she will immediately pull the trigger.
Or perhaps... she could just pull the trigger now. That doesn't seem like a bad choice either.
She can tell from her opponent's eyes that she is thinking the exact same thing.
What's going on? The southern zone's blown up? You mean, the entire wall? It could only be done from the inside... Dammit!
Hurry, let's back them up—
Is this the signal for us to evacuate?
Uh... The Doctor never told me anything... but since the Sarkaz are gone, I guess our mission is over?
You two should go on ahead.
Who's there?
You're with Rhodes Island...
I'm taking over from here.
Captain, the Sarkaz evacuated! This is the perfect opportunity! Should we head inside or...?
Let's go.
Horn does not take her hand off her crossbow. Her glare closely follows Mandragora as she leaves.

A single bolt. From this distance, that's all it would take. All she has to do is pull the trigger, and her opponent's chest would be torn open, splattering blood everywhere, just like what happened to Cello.
Having retrofitted this crossbow herself, Horn knows its limits very well, but they're so close to each other. With the two of them now within a hair's distance...
Horn, they're gone.
I was really worried about controlling myself, Horn... Thanks for holding me back. You're right. We shouldn't fight Dublinn here.
If we actually started fighting each other, the Sarkaz would've found us.
I'm surprised Mandragora could hold herself back, though. She was hounding us like crazy... What are they doing?
What is Dublinn doing?
Horn refuses to analyze this question. Her fingertips are still from the boiling blood. Why didn't she pull the trigger?
Why didn't Mandragora raise her hand?
These voices are clashing in her chest. She can feel a clear sense of regret.
But she can't say that.
That's not what her men want to hear.
Forget Mandragora. She's not our objective.
Okay, Horn. What should we do now? Follow Dublinn inside?
Yes.
Everyone, we're cancelling our original plan. From here on out, we will stay dark.
Blake, you'll continue to be point man. If you run into any Sarkaz guards, take them down. Robben, you'll follow Blake and provide fire support when necessary.
Everyone else, follow me.
Do you see the opening up ahead? Our Dublinn friends left us that. We should take advantage of it when they're dealing with our enemies.
Remember, we came as a team of five, so we'll leave as a team of five. No... We'll leave this Sarkaz prison with even more of our friends.
Watch my hand signal—
Move out!
*Cough*... *cough*... What was that explosion? Miss Closure. That wasn't you, was it?
Error—Error—Restarting—
Err, it wasn't her. She wouldn't have blown up her own drone.
Doctor, is this part of your plan? It's a little... flashy, isn't it?

opts: Nope.
Maybe?

lines.x: Doctor, is it...?
Look, someone's walking past the dust! It's... It's another Sarkaz! Rock-17—
Again with the weapons? Not to mention... More fresh meat?
Doctor, is this how you always greet your heroes?
Is this Sarkaz one of yours...?
Miss Rockrock, please keep your weapon trained on her.
C'mon, little bunny. It's been aaages! Are you really so heartless? That hurts... it cuts deep, you know?
Mr. Feist, please check for any Originium explosives in our immediate vicinity.
No time. We have people to rescue...
Aw man, what a shame. Looks like we don't have time for proper hellos.
C'mere, Heidi.
You're here for me?
Hey now, can't you at least sound a little more enthusiastic? We only just met, and it's not like you're the biggest fan of Doctor anyway.
If it wasn't for that stinking old bitch... the one you love ever so much, asking me to come get you, I wouldn't be here either.
Ask any Sarkaz here, I don't come cheap.
Kal'tsit sent you?
A Sarkaz mercenary... an explosives expert...
Are you... W?
Whoa, she told you about me? Should I be breaking down in tears of joy? Look at that, out of all my old friends running around, there's actually somebody who still cares about me.
This way, through the smoke! Follow the light of the fire!
Devil...!
What's this? An egg mixer?
Did you think you could hurt a Sarkaz mercenary with a toy like this?
S-Stay back!
Calm down. Over here, behind me.
This one's on our side... for now.
Your side? Better not be too sure of yourself on that one.
W... it would be wise if you made an effort to be easier to work with. That is, if you hope to get any information out of me.
That's a pretty roundabout threat there...
Say... you wouldn't happen to be Kal'tsit's illegitimate daughter, would you?
What are you—
Nah, you two look nothing alike. Plus, how could that icy old hag have any kids?
Screw it. No way I could ever get buddy buddy with a lady like you.
Suits me just fine.
Don't you worry, I won't be here long. I know how much my head's worth in merc circles, and I'm not in a generous mood.
Alright, there.
Bombs... you buried more bombs. Did you blow up the other paths?
Where are your men? Aren't they still fighting the Sarkaz in the plant?
Do you really want to know?
Explaining the whole deal would be a huge pain. Let's skip right to the good part.
They're all dead.
Dead?
Did you think it would be easy to scoop you out of there? I lost most of the Londinium recruits I picked up the past few months in that nasty old factory.
So this had better be worth it. Can we go now?
Let me do a head count...
Alright, everyone in the southern plant escaped.
I was hired to snag you, not an entire congregation. Tell Kal'tsit I'm charging double for this mess.
Bring your hooded monster and let's—what are you standing around for?
W, was it you who blew up this path?
Duh. Not about to give our pursuers any chances.
But Feist and his team just left.

opts: They went to rescue people.
Hail them, Closure.

lines.x: Oh, come on... getting lost like this, at your age?
What's the situation, Blake?
They're down.
Good. Keep moving.
Horn, to your left!
Dublinn... I'll have your head!
Not Dublinn here. Not that you'll get the chance to wallow in your disappointment.
Another? How many Sarkaz bodies have we seen?
All Dublinn?
Looks like it.
Horn, I don't know how to put this, but...
Dublinn saved us a lot of effort.
There's no shame in admitting it. How long have you been with me here in Sudean? We need to use every means at our disposal if we want to survive.
Come on, left door here.
Ready?
Clear.
Good. Now the right side.
Watch the corners, Robben. I don't want any surprises.
Roger.
Go!
*Cough* G-Go away, devil! We're not talking!
Found them.
Y-You're...
Friends.
I never thought... we heard sounds of fighting, saw the flicker of Arts... I thought it was some new devil torture method.
Sorry we couldn't get here earlier.
Can you stand, soldier?
Soldier... haven't heard that in a long time.
You an officer? You've got nerve to come here—
What are you doing? We're here to save you!
Save me? Why? So I can get tricked again? So I can die in your next plot with the foreigners?
In your dreams, 'sir'! We'd rather die here!
Very well.
Robben, your crossbow.
Horn? What are you—
Ammo is precious and not to be wasted.
But these are compatriots, loyal soldiers of Victoria.
If their last wish is to die here, then we'll grant it.
You'll really kill me?
Yes.
You'll die as you wish in this dark room, forgotten by Londinium.
You'll turn to bleached bones in this old abandoned factory, until the dust and dirt bury you.
Or, you could do something different with this crossbow.
You could take it, come with us, and fight your way out.
Perhaps you'll still get your wish and die trying.
But you'll have a chance to see the night sky, breathe the air full with the smell of rust and oil—
And think about your loved ones, breathing that same air.
I swear on my life that they'll remember what you gave.
Yes... they... they could still be alive...
Have you made your choice? Time's flying.
I'll go with you. Give me a weapon.
What about the others? Are you coming too?
Good. Blake, you have seven... nine new men.
Tell me your names before we move out, soldiers.
Closure, have you made contact with the Self-Salvation Corps team?
Hold on... I just got this drone restarted... it's different from the other one...
This isn't good.
Heidi, lead the other civilians out of here. The 3rd regiment will be waiting—
W?
Hey, all the mines I left behind got blown up! Do you know what that means? Means a lotta guys are chasing us right now.
Using up my men wasn't enough for you, huh? You gotta use up my bombs too?

opts: Five minutes.
We can't wait any longer.

lines.x: Alright, five minutes it is.
Closure, get in touch with Feist and tell him that he and Rockrock need to be back within 5 minutes.
Doctor, we'll have to join the battle.
Bill, be honest with me... have you put on weight? You're heavier than I thought...
Hehe... how about this?
Hey, that's better.
What did you do? Some magic trick?
Ha, just a change of posture... *cough*
A-Alright, just stay put, don't open your wounds.
When I saw you lying in that pool of blood, I thought...
Thought I was dead?
Ha... those devils don't got the skills... it's just... ouch...
Take a break, don't talk anymore.
Have you found an exit, Rockrock? The Sarkaz must have done something with these rooms... I feel like we're going around in circles.
I heard the sound of fighting a few minutes ago.
The exit should be around here...
Nrgh...
Never mind what I just said, Bill. Keep talking so you don't fall asleep.
Remember the reward you asked for? After that last win?
Er... a fruit can?
A FRUIT CAN? You said you wanted a mechanical metal crab to give to your niece in the City!
Yeah, I was hungry... lucky you've got a good memory.
Good memory? I didn't sleep at all the next few days, working on that thing between missions...
You'd better hang in there after all the effort I put in!
*Cough* Alright, boss... whatever you say.
Footsteps!
Oh no! Sarkaz—
Where did they go?
There's a blood trail. They can't have gone far.
We should hurry.
Er...
Come on, Captain, I'll take the other side.
Sigh... look at me, can't even walk without two people helping me.
Don't say that. We're family, and we look out for each other.
Captain, they're catching up!
Hold them off, Johnny! Gabby, you've got the grenades, right? Give us some cover!
Roger!
Rockrock, has Rock-17 found a way out? We're counting on it!
Right-turn here!
Alright, to the right!
Wait... who's talking? Rock-17? Y-You can talk?
What's that, some dumb engineering joke?
Took me ages to hack this drone's system... I didn't change anything, ok? The system was too simple.
...Closure?
Yes, it's me. I didn't have a chance to install voice data onto your drone, so I have to synthesize it... not cute enough?
Well... it actually sounds rather like you.
Where do we go from here?
Left-turn... wait, no, there are Sarkaz there. I mean enemy Sarkaz! Go back—
There are even more enemies to the rear.
Well then... let me think...
Right, let's go upwards!
Huh?
Don't you have that flying gear? Er, I mean grappling hook! There's a vent above you that should allow a man to pass through...
Should?
We have wounded here. Gotta be sure.
Well... I'll need Rockrock to take control of the drone for a moment...
Alright.
What do we do?
Rockrock... do you really trust her?
Do you have a better plan?
Captain, they're coming! I can see their leader!
Quick, open the vent!
Gotcha! Rockrock, aim!
Rock-17—
Is it open?
Almost.
Again!
Rock...
It missed! H-How did that sword...
Finally found a way out? Isn't it a little late?
I'll hold him off! Quick—
Blind courage isn't worth much in battle.
Johnny!
Rockrock, the vent!
I... I can't...
I've got two grenades left. Even the Sarkaz can't—
What the... the grenade got cut in half?!
Captain, this is no ordinary Sarkaz—
Gabby!
I'm just an ordinary mercenary.
Did you really think you could beat Manfred or Theresis?
Pretty naive.
Sarkaz... did you think all I had was grenades?
Grenades? Smoke bombs is more like it.
Bombs that can hurt a Sarkaz don't look like that.
Feist, Rockrock, you hear me? I've recalibrated... but you only have one chance. Hold on tight!
Rockrock—
It worked! We can go up now!
You first!
No, you're with BIll, you go first—
Alright.
You're not getting through, Sarkaz!
I'll protect my family... you won't hurt them again!
Rock-17!
Self-destruct drone?
Pathetic...
Captain... go...
Tell me, Feline... was it really worth it? Losing that many to save one?
Every one of them called out for their captain with their last breaths.
Will you take responsibility for their sacrifice?
...I would rather none of them died.
Hold on, Rockrock!
Huh?
The grappling hook again? Smart, but not smart enough.
You need better tricks to survive in battle.
We'll meet again, children... you don't know it, but there's no way you'll leave this inferno.
Are they here, Closure?
Four minutes, fifty seconds.
Countdown?
We...
Amiya!
They're here!
Feist? Just the three of you? The other two...
They won't be back.
We need to get out of here, now!
There's a really dangerous Sarkaz after us, and there are a lot of them... it's an ambush!
None of us would have made it, if not for Closure...
They knew we'd go there, and they know you're here. They're just waiting... it's a trap! We have to go, before it's too late!
It's too late.
Damn it, this is my last mine, and the biggest one.

opts: Another last one?
How many last ones is that?

lines.x: Stop bothering me, Doctor. Yes, I have one bomb left. Guess who it's for?

opts: Are you nervous?
Got your hands full?

lines.x: You didn't notice the explosion sounded kinda weird? I guess there's really nothing under that tin head of yours.
The guy didn't fall into my trap at all. He avoided it and disabled the Originium circuits.
Need me to say it even clearer? No, it didn't take me too much effort to set up the mines...
But the dragon lady was the last one to put all my mines out of commission.
Talulah can't be here, can she? There's no way...
Hey, what's with the face, bunny? You've got her all locked up in...
Damn it, Doctor... damn you and damn that Kal'tsit!
Congratulations, Doctor, you're now second on my list of people to bomb.

opts: This is not good news.
Thanks.

lines.x: W, there's a lot of intel that we haven't shared. We need to get out of here...
Get out? Easy for you to say.
Be glad it's not Talulah here. I don't smell the arrogant stench of Draco fire. But...
Surround the place!
Theta, Iota, block the path on the other side!
They got so many men in so little time?
Oh no! Doctor, Lady Heidi and the others are still inside!
Sorry, Amiya...
Save that for when we get out.
Very well.
Doctor, let me... W? Why are you grabbing me like that?
Save your strength, bunny. Look at the gang you've got here. Artificer, wounded, garbage... who can fight?
Y-You're right, but we'll do our best...
W-We won't be a burden.
Save your strength for running. You'll wanna be fast.
Sigh... I didn't want to say this. It's really not my style.
But I need Heidi alive. There's something I need to ask the old woman.
Go. Take your people, and pick up your trash.
W!
Huh?
Good luck. We'll be waiting.
You're still here? Need me to blow you up?
Finally. Some peace.
She talks like we're actually friends.
As for you...
Wanna keep blasting? At this rate, there won't be much left to bomb.
What's this? A rock? Some kind of lame joke?
Congratulations, you've pissed me off.
I was hoping to be a little frugal today.
Why don't you come out? I promise I'll leave something of you that's recognizable.
Well, that was close. Only two bombs away from hitting me.
You've grown again, W.
Wait, what—
You?!
Didn't think I would ever see you surprised like this.
I gave you a hint, after all.
That rock? Very funny.
I thought...
Who did you think would come? Manfred?
I'm thinking Theresis must be getting senile if he let you live. You even get to roam around Londinium like this?
I thought you guys were long dead, since I haven't heard anything from you in ages.
Well... you know what they say about assuming? See, you were right. I've become sentimental, almost forgot we're mercs.
Your detonator hand slowed down, W.
And your hand isn't trembling this time.
What about those mercenaries you brought from Reunion? Did you really come alone?
Why would I bring them? So that they could get bought out by the Regent, like you?
Looks like you care about them. No wonder I didn't see any familiar faces.
Speak for yourself. I saw lots of old friends in Londinium.
You really like headhunting under my nose, don't you?
Let me guess, Schwab is dead, isn't he? You killed him, didn't you, Hoederer? He knew you longer than he knew me, right?
Did your hand tremble when you killed an old friend? One who carried you on his back for an entire day, dodging dozens of Theresis's killers?
You're remembering it wrong. He was gone by the Battle for Rhodes Island.
Alright, you've always had a better memory than me for the people we fought with.
I've always wanted to ask... does it make you feel better to remember the dead?
I don't think so. I can see it in your eyes. Your dreams haunt you now more than ever.
I've known you for as long as you've known me.
You're only trying to hide your own feelings when move to provoke me like that.
Ha, like you'd know.
In the old days, you would have thrown out those useless resistance fighters as bait.
If someone told me that W volunteered to be the rear guard, I would have told them to find a funnier joke.
Did your time in Chernobog drive you mad, W?
Did Talulah burn your brains out, or are you seeing the shadows of the dead?
The mad never admit their madness, right? How do you know I'm the one who's mad?
Schwab's head isn't enough to buy Theresis's trust. What was the price you had to pay?
Could it have been her? Don't tell me...
Don't even say it.
Oh? There's a reaction.
We've done all we could.
You were too slow, W.
Did I hear that right? Are you looking for an excuse for your screw-ups? Who was it who told me he had a plan for everything?
When do you think I lost my eye?
You don't mean that Ines...
Yes, she's dead.
Again?
I'm not...
Mercenaries pay a price for breaching trust. I've paid mine.
We're out.
We've been lucky, haven't we?
The Sarkaz got drawn to the explosions in the south. The ones guarding the interrogation room got taken out by Dublinn.
Nothing good comes of relying on luck in battle.
Ha. Did you think you would have lived this long, if not for lady luck?
W-Who's she? Did they escape from the interrogation room too? Are they friends?
Enemies.
(Aim.)
(But hold your fire.)
Can't talk without your crossbow raised, soldier? Don't think I didn't know you only got out by following us.
You want me to thank you? Then you'd better take a trip with me to the hardest prison in Londinium.
I'll give you credit.
You're not like the nobles that seep oil from every pore.
They're the ones who kneel before anyone to cling to their way of life. Sarkaz or Dublinn... it makes no difference to them.
You, though... you just won't give up. You're like flies, getting in my way no matter where I go.
That's my line.
Why you...!
*Cough* *cough* Mandragora...
Your friend's in pretty bad shape.
Same to those soldiers behind you.
Looks like we're both in a hurry.
It's a shame that I don't have time for you today.
Suits me just fine.
Make way.
Let's go.
By the way, soldier—
Huh?
You had better not die at the hands of the Sarkaz...
Before I have the chance to impale you with my rock.
Whew... they're gone.
Good. Save your ammo for the Sarkaz.
Do you mean...?
Don't you think it's too easy?
Whoever Dublinn took from the interrogation room, I doubt the Sarkaz would let them go so easily.
Or us, for that matter.
Do you have everyone, Lady Heidi?
Yes, Amiya. They're all here.
Careful!
Let the weakest go first. We'll take care of any pursuing mercenaries.
Feist, can you still fight?
Of course.
Hang on tight, Bill. Things could get bumpy.
More enemies ahead! They must have come in from another entrance!
Target found!
Over here! Get them!
Hey, don't run too fast. Got to keep a pair of eyes in the back of your head when moving.
A few got away.
Still not enough?
Get that caster first. Don't let him cast any Arts on our wounded.
Thank goodness you are back.
We aren't late to the party, are we?

opts: Great.
What luck.

lines.x: Whew... Doctor, you didn't tell me there was only one fighter meeting up with us.

opts: One is enough.
There's more than one.

lines.x: You know, Doctor, the more I learn about Rhodes Island, the more mysterious it seems.
But at the end of the day, the most mysterious one is always you.
You always have a card up your sleeve, don't you?
On this point, we're the same.
It looks like the Sarkaz have been drawn over here. But we didn't see many souls on the way.
The ones holding off the enemies inside... I'm worried about them.
I'm worried about W...
W? The one who made such an impression on all those operators at Chernobog?
Er, yeah.
Rhodes Island really IS filled with talent.
We have a... temporary understanding.
Doctor, I felt something eerie in her gaze when she said she was looking for Lady Heidi.

opts: What did you feel, Amiya?
Isn't she always like that?

lines.x: Her emotions can be unusual, but there are times that she doesn't try to hide them. Her eyes don't lie at times like that.
The last time I saw that look from her was when she was yelling at me for the codes to stop the core city.
Is she still hiding something?

opts: Ask her when she comes back.
She's W. She'll be back.

lines.x: Alright... I trusted her once, I'll trust her again.
Huff... huff...
You're bleeding a lot.
You've got a few holes in you.
You should have hidden another bomb on yourself, like you used to. That way, you'd at least have a chance to take me down with you.
*Cough*... Hoederer...
How much is my head worth? Enough to buy a small house in Londinium?
Sorry, W. The Regent has better things to do than remember the name of a random merc.
Ha... did you say that to Ines too? Before she gave her life for your little dream?
Still talking big, eh?
What were you expecting? A big laugh and a standing ovation for your dumb shit? No tears on my face, Hoederer. That's not like me.
Who's the dumb one? You're on the ground, not me.
Like I said, I'm just... a little surprised.
So surprised you missed multiple chances to maim me?
Now that's not like you, W. What made you lose your cool?
Let's see... how about an old friend coming back from the dead and trying to put his sword through me?
I can believe that.
W... who is this old friend you're talking about?
That's what I don't like about you... You always think you can see right through me, same as her.
Too bad for you, I'm right again.
It's been a while since you snuck into Londinium. There aren't too many distractions like this in here. What did you see in the city?
You're the one following Theresis, not me. Have you not kowtowed to his traitor throne enough times? Still can't see through his tricks?
So you knew.
Stop playing this game of you-know I-know. Look like a mercenary for once, Hoederer.
I can't let you leave here alive, W.
Of all the old friends that I've killed... you're probably the one who could best understand the choice I'm making.
Now who's indecisive?
I'm not taking my eyes off you. I want to see the Sarkaz who had the nerve to put his sword through me.
Alright then.
Goodbye, W.
...Huh?
Something deflected my sword... a dagger? Where did it come from?
Don't move.
It's been a long time, Ascalon.
Hey...
Same to you. Don't move.
*Cough* Or what?
Wait, you came to save me, right?
Don't tell me you came to help him? Don't you know what Theresis did to her—
Maybe work on your aim? I just barely dodged that, what with my injuries and all.
Too bad.
You know you can't kill me now, right?
You've been my boss, and I've been yours. But there's someone here who's been boss to both of us. And she's clearly not in a good mood.
I know. Our heads could drop off our shoulders at any moment.
I have nothing else to say.
Each word will make you bleed a little faster.
I know, thanks for the concern.
Looks like you'll have to accept a little failure if you don't want to die here together, 'Captain' Hoederer.
So you mean to say that Hoederer has failed?
Yes, General. I saw it with my own eyes. He was gravely wounded in the battle just now.
And he was in contact with another Sarkaz woman, aside from his longtime mercenary acquaintance?
Right. But she's... highly skilled at concealment, and moves too quickly.
I was anxious about getting too close. Couldn't shake the feeling she'd notice me with one wrong step.
Wise choice. You wouldn't have made it back alive otherwise.
That said, I'm surprised the Confessarius let Babel's assassin go free. Was that an oversight, or their intent?
...No matter.
Tell me about Hoederer. Where is he?
He's still marshaling the remaining mercenaries, and about to resume pursuing the escaped convicts.
Have him return. Those convicts will not stay free forever.
General?
As you said, his wounds are no laughing matter.
Forgive me, but why do you still trust Hoederer so much?
I've had you with him this whole time.
Correct me if I'm wrong, but you've never once reported any misdeeds from him.
But he used to work for Babel!
It was the mercenaries under his watch that screwed up, and it was his old friend that incited them! Don't you think it's too risky to keep him in such a key position, General?
You've seen for yourself today. How does one Victorian regard another?
Urgh...
And how much more difficult do you think it is for one Sarkaz to trust another?
We've had everything stolen from us, been forced to grow accustomed to fighting each other for the right to survive.
And now, we've obtained a new opportunity.
You tell me, do you want to go back to monitoring a possible Sarkaz spy, or will you accompany me in eliminating the remaining Victorian threats?
General, we choose to go with you.
*pant*... *pant*... Did we shake them?
We can't let our guards down yet, Mr. Feist.
I know... *huff*... I know.
...Captain.
What is it?
Your face is splattered in blood.
Blood?! Hey, Bill... Bill?
Nrgh...
...Oh, it's just my own blood. No need to panic, then.
Come on, Bill, you could breathe a little louder! I don't want to have spent all this effort porting a dead man back.
Then think about... swinging your spanner gentler.
You're always... hitting me... in the head...
I hear you. You're the boss today.
Hold on a little longer... we'll be right back home soon. Right? I'm not going blind, am I?
Eugh, this blood from my forehead's getting in my eyes a little... Rockrock, eyes out. How much left to our target rendezvous?
A little over 300 meters and we're there.
It's Team 6 on guard here, right? I could really use a hand from them. Bill, old bud, you're getting harder to carry by the second.
...How long have we gone without a signal from the surface team?
Didn't the Doctor say they'd only contact us after they reach that last RDV?
No, I mean the Self-Salvation Corps, at the one before that.
We had an agreement that they'd keep us up to date with the surface situation every half hour.
Shit, I wasn't tracking the time! How long's it been?
...We need to go up and see.
No!
If the Sarkaz have already discovered our last rendezvous, then the Doctor's squad is in danger. You're clearly worried sick about Morgan and Dagda too.
A... And you too!
Worst comes to worst, Vina, I go peek, and you stay here. Suppose somehow something's happened, you scarper further in...
Indra, I can't always keep cowering behind you all. First our mentor, then you and the Glasgow Gang, now the Doctor—
—I've cowered for far too long, and I know it. Dagda wasn't talking nonsense. If you're always tucked away in the shadows, you gradually lose the courage to directly face the sun.
She's trying to nark you! You know what she's dying for? You, taking her to storm the Palace, and finding that what's-his-gent to do him in!
And you're itching to go back to the Glasgow Gang. Don't you understand her point of view?
I... Like hell I don't, but that's the bloody thing. I understand, and that's why...
You're scared she'll end up killing herself, the way she rushes it.
You can bloody imagine, can't you? She's got a death wish in my eyes, the way she acts!
...You need to trust her.
She'll be willing to live for our sake... and the sake of so many, even if it comes with enormous pain.
She's an outstanding knight, and a warrior of Victoria. She has everything in common with the countless others who struggle to live, and fight on for Victoria.
And I... I have to fight with them. Not because I'm in some special position, but because we're all 'native' to this land.
Vina...
Think of that commander, Lady Clovisia. Would she abandon her soldiers, run off just to keep herself safe?
I'm not familiar with her... but I don't imagine she would. Those soldiers really trust in her.
Then in your eyes, how much do I pale in comparison?
Like bloody hell you pale at all!
Then follow me. Together, we'll make sure that our comrades-in-arms have a safe trip home.
We finally get to leave this stinking place soon.
Are you alright, 'Spy'?
...Whoof... *huff*... I'm fine, Mandragora.
The last few times I've heard the word 'spy' it's been from a Sarkaz thinking up some fresh new punishment. I almost forgot it was my codename.
Then pick a more tasteful one next time, if you have the chance. 'The Elocutionist' and all that lot had good ones, and they kicked the bucket without leaving so much as a smear.
Haha...
Save your strength for now. The Leader still needs the Sarkaz intel you found.
You mean... The Shard...
No, don't say it.
Not like I don't want to hear, but... we need the Leader's approval. Our soldiers are all clean sorts. Don't go giving them too big a burden to carry.
I understand... Mandragora... you've always been such a good girl.
Don't sweet-talk me like 'The Bandit' did. Sick of it. They're all gone and now I'm stuck here alone in this shithole having to worry about the Leader.
At least you got back quick. While you were gone, that Harmonie bastard was glued to the Leader's side day and night, and I was terrified that... that...
Terrified... that what?
That the Leader would get too close to those noble sorts, and that someday, she'd have no more use for us.
The Leader, eh... *sigh*...
Ma'am, I've ascertained the others' positions. They're still in the same place, watching the door for the Sarkaz. Are we linking up with them right now?
Mm. Get the paramedics going. 'The Spy' needs immediate treatment.
Right, one more thing. Find a Messenger and notify Count Mourning outside the city immediately. We have to ensure 'The Spy' meets the Leader as soon as possible.
Count Mourning? You... involved him for the Leader?
Tch. Times like these, we have no choice but to hope the nobles do something to help. Funny kind of joke, isn't it?
*cough* *cough*... You... You've grown well...
That's not like you, 'Spy'. You're speaking too much rubbish. Save the rest for when you're not ragged enough to drop dead at a stiff breeze.
...Hold it.
What is it, Horn? We have a visual on the exit already.
Mind the perimeter.
Three o'clock!
Horn, it's Sarkaz!
Robben, are you still fine for ammo?
Ha... haha... sorry, Horn. Truth is, I've been hiding an explosive shell on me.
All this time, I've had it in my head that if I fall into the enemy's hands at some point again... I'll be taking this shell with me to my last stand.
...Shame, we improvised that shell ourselves.
How's that got anything to do with—
And I'm very sorry, but I'm not inclined to give you that opportunity.
Eh? My shell—
Looks like the power of a homemade one falls a bit short after all.
But... but he's unscathed, how?
That Sarkaz...
That Sarkaz is their beloved General.
We finally meet. An improvised shell is hardly a suitable greeting though.
I know you've met your share of Victorian soldiers, Sarkaz. After all, a great deal of them died by your hands. I imagine their spirits are practically lining up to crowd your dreams.
No need to be so self-effacing, White Wolf of Victoria.
Even to the Sarkaz, you qualify as a heroic warrior.
As I recall, the captain who rendered Kazdel a sea of flames once more, two hundred years ago, was your ancestor.
Are you some monster who's lived for centuries too?
I had no chance to witness that war.
But listen to the way you talk about it. It's no different from your kin who feed on others' lives to prolong their own.
'Those stained in Sarkaz blood must pay that blood back, thousands of times over.'
Where do you think we came from, Victorian? Just who runs in fear at the restless souls circling Londinium's skies day and night, murmuring their blood feuds?
Did you know? I became a professional soldier precisely because I was fed up with justifying this war we're in.
Come, Sarkaz.
The words of the deceased lingering about my ears are more than you'll ever hear.
And I promised them... I promised them this.
I will not fall until I see my people out of this place alive.
It's so strange, Doctor. We definitely shook off the Sarkaz warriors from before...

opts: But they're still coming without end.
It's like they know our position.

lines.x: Closure, are our communications being traced?
Not a chance, Amiya, I guarantee it! I set up triple-layer encryption on our transmitters! If they had the tech for that, then Theresis would've breached the landship a hundred times over by now!
...Doctor, I believe Closure.
They must be using some other method then.
Miss Amiya, we're a stone's throw from the rendezvous point!
As long as we link up with Team 6, we can get in contact with the subterranean support unit. Once we're underground, the Sarkaz pursuing us will be out of luck.
No, don't go that way.
Huh? Why... wait, what? Miss Siege, what are you doing here?
Weren't you under...
Team 6 is...
Team 6 is what? Why do you look so... erk, Miss Indra, have you seen all that blood on your hands?!
Your soldiers, they... all gave their lives.
Th-That can't be right! Team 6 is still ahead of us, how could they have run into the Sarkaz?
We don't have time to analyze how.
Doctor, it wasn't the Sarkaz force pursuing us that killed Team 6's soldiers.
You mean...
The enemy they faced had terrifying Originium Arts... It was more of a massacre than a battle. Hardly any of them had even drawn their weapons.
You sure it was Originium Arts?
I don't believe that!
I saw this red bug thing tear the soldiers' chests apart. Vina, you saw it too! That was a genuine monster!
...So the perpetrator is no ordinary Sarkaz.
We have to change course. Mr. Feist, are there any other routes we can take?
Yes. But we don't have any support waiting on it. If we run into Sarkaz forces...
We'll be in for another hard fight.
Find cover!
Horn, what the hell is this?!
What did that Sarkaz throw at us, a bomb? None of us even saw it—
...Originium Arts.
Eyes up!
I... I just saw something red flash by in midair!
Retreat!
It's like a thunderbolt or something! That's Originium Arts?!
These... abominable devils!
Still won't come out?
Your war, your battle, your fight, it can't be this game of runabout you're playing, can it White Wolf?
Five aerial blood streaks at once?
Horn, we have no chance of—
No.
No matter how strong the Arts... he's only one person.
What did Victoria do in the past, to prevail over the Sarkaz again and again?
You mean...
How many bolts do we have left?
Cover me.
Yes, sir!
A pitiful act of defiance—
Now... is our chance.
Counting on an Arts bombardment to make your escape?
I'll give you a rude awakening.
What I said still stands. So long as my soldiers remain here, I will not flee.
Not bad after all, White Wolf. I see you have the bravado to reverse course and charge me.
Such a shame then...
––Rrgh!
Whoever told you that a Sarkaz depends solely on Arts in a fight?
Even wielding no more than a sword—I can still defeat every last one of you.
Beating me... may not be so difficult...
But making me fall will... not be so easy.
How very brave of you.
It's thanks to that valor of yours that I can end this battle that much quicker—
The instant he thrusts his sword out, the soldier before Manfred abruptly vanishes.
She seems to practically dissolve and melt through the wall behind her, leaving not even a ripple.
These Arts are unfamiliar...
Vigilance!
...A dagger from thin air?
No, not from thin air...
You react very fast.
I'm afraid I'd have one more hole in my chest otherwise.
Target found! Take aim!
What the hell? They vanished again?
Th-They're too fast! I can't land a shot!
Shields up, up! Quick! Shit, are they able to blink?
Blink? No such Arts could be cast without leaving even a trace!
Or is this... a diversion?
Don't let them fool you! All men, cast precision aside! Attack at once!
So long as the enemy is still in front of us, they have no escape!
Have we... hit them?
General, there's no body.
But the ground is shot to hell... Where can they still be hiding?
General, behind you!
Show yourself, and your life is mine!
Really?
Manfred, you put it very well, but unfortunately... you were only half right.
Half?
Sarkaz do not depend solely on Arts to fight—
—but in no world do Sarkaz wield swords alone.
Aarrghh!
The ground shouldn't be this soft—this place is going to swallow us! I-I'm losing my grip on my blade!
General, you're sinking too?!
...Nobody move, and nobody panic.
The dagger is his staff. These are Arts, and nothing more than Arts.
You're very calm-headed. Good... you've saved your men's lives.
However...
Rgh!
The ground's... nearing my stomach...
My sword...
With your swordsmanship, blocking my attacks would be a simple task.
But the more you struggle, the quicker the ground swallows you.
Is this how you want to go... this self-destruction?
Urgh... *cough* *cough*...
General—
No one come near!
Or, you could cease all movements, same as your men.
You want me to wait as you run my heart through?
It'd be tough, killing you.
*cough* *cough*... *pant*...
I see now. You're not actually here to take my life.
I never imagined that outside of Ascalon, Rhodes Island would have yet another apex Sarkaz assassin.
I'm no assassin.
Unless it's called for, I have zero desire to harm any Sarkaz at all.
But here you are, abetting our enemies!
Are these Victorians... your enemies?
What I see is a group of bruised, weary warriors, on the soil of their own home, struggling painfully just to live.
You and Theresis, on the other hand, are turning the Sarkaz into the enemy of all.
I had entertained having a talk with your people, if I had the chance.
It's a sorry thing, that we should first meet on the battlefield—
—Rhodes Island Elite Operator.
He's gone again? Ngh—the ground's back to normal!
A hair's breadth.
I was on the cusp of capturing him. His Arts are all too exceptional... He can induce hallucinations without influencing our nervous systems.
There are marks of energy transformation on the ground. Could it be...?
General, they're all gone! Those Victoria soldiers, I mean—
Keh... He would visit me and stall for time, just to extricate those half-dead soldiers one by one?
Are... Are we pursuing them, then?
Do you think we can?
I may have called it a hair's breadth, but against a Caster of that level, a hair's breadth is tantamount to guaranteed failure.
Dammit! We were set to clean out all the rebel forces by today! Now a mercenary's gone traitor AND some mystery Caster's held us back!
It's Rhodes Island... This is all because of Rhodes Island.
We missed our chance to round them up in their entirety, but their losses can't be light, can they?
They had their chance to hide in the dark, but now they've been driven front and center. For them, revealing their forces is already a colossal tactical defeat.
An assassin, an expert spacial illusionist Caster, and the as-yet unseen Banshee... How many cards does Rhodes Island still have up their sleeve?
General, what are your plans?
...Let us go. Before we deal with Rhodes Island, we first need to put the discontented clown in its place.
This should be far enough for now.
...Is this outside of the factory district?
You... cut open space with your dagger?
Don't always trust your senses. Tricking your eyes is much easier than influencing space.
You've...
You've rescued me, and not just once. My eyes might not show me the truest answers, but my senses never lie.
Just who are you?
Misery. I'm Misery of Rhodes Island.
Rhodes Island?
I'm sorry...
You'll get a chance to hear a full explanation from Bagpipe in due time.
Bagpipe?
She's a coworker at this point.
Not to mention... the last friend my best friend ever made.
Sounds like I've been quite a bit out of the loop.
But before I lose myself to the joy that Bagpipe made it out, allow me to once again thank you and Rhodes Island.
Let's get out of here first. Chances are we'll be meeting again soon enough.
Manfred's almost worked out the truth of my Originium Arts. He could be coming after us any moment now.
...No. I imagine he'll be busy with something else right now.
You mean Dublinn.
County Hillock's Mandragora... There's no way she's leaving this factory now.
Are you giving yourself grief for not being able to personally avenge your comrades?
I... I'm just lamenting.
Myself for standing here, regretting that I missed the opportunity to kill her...
Or her, for being long since beyond saving.
That's odd. This is what you agreed with them, right? Why aren't they here to back us up?
I—argh!
What the hell is it—?
Sarkaz! It's Sarkaz! We have to run—
...Were we spotted?
No. Not like this. 'Spy', we have to go—
Only ten meters to that sliding door there, right? Just ten meters, and we're right out, and...
A... Alright, let's run...
Just like we used to, you pulling me. We only had to run fast enough that those noble kids couldn't catch us...
Grotesques! Get down, block them!
Bastards! Can't even rely on you in a time like this?
Mandragora...
What? Didn't I tell you to stay quiet for now?
I can't keep walking. You go.
What...? Can't you see? I've been doing all this just to...
To save me? Or to gain favor with the Leader?
Is there a difference? What are you talking about? We're just five meters away, our people are right outside!
They're gone.
What?
Mandragora... The Leader... she's long since abandoned us...
'Spy'!
Bastard, bastard—stones, go! Skewer them, impale every one of those Sarkaz!
You'll be alright, 'Spy', it's just an arrow, we're three meters away, even if I have to carry you—
...Just go.
If you can make it out...
Don't... Don't head for the Leader.
...Did you just die?
Who said you could die so easily...? I...
Cillian, Cillian... you bloody disgrace! I'm not letting you die here, you stand up for me, stand up!
No need to work yourself up.
You won't live much longer either, Mandragora. You and your old Taran friend will meet again before you know it.
Ha... hahahaha!
Manfred, you cost me another eleven lives.
You don't know the half... you don't know anything of what it took to kill that many Sarkaz.
...This is exactly why you must die here.
Perfect... drop yourselves right in my lap.
I'll crush every one of you into paste, no prisoners.
Hfff... *pant*...
Your energy is spent, Mandragora.
...Monsters.
You Sarkaz... all of you, monsters.
We're far from strangers to that sort of address.
When I hear it from an opponent crawling on the ground, I'm inclined to take it as a compliment.

*cough* *cough*...
*cough* *cough* *cough*.

Sarkaz, you're... just like those noble lot... disgusting.
That Arts...
It's all natural-born—I could never learn it in a lifetime...
You have the nerve...
To treat Oripathy as a blessing?
Countless Sarkaz are burdened by this curse from birth, of festering bodies, organs oozing—you see us only as pests spreading blight, drive us out from the cities.

How many Sarkaz each day become crystals scattering to the wind, cannot even be laid to rest in the soil of their home, simply because they wanted to see another sunrise?
Yet you tell me...
You envy this power? Is that your place to say, Victorian?
You're calling me a Victorian. You really know the best way to insult me, devil.
Do you know... why I avoid Oripathy?
The Sarkaz in the barrens... at least, before they die... they get to see the sun come up.
Us, we live in the cracks of the city, where a speck of black stone pokes out of your skin... and the very next day, you're food in the sewers for the metal crabs.

In Victorian eyes, we weak little Tarans... don't even amount to what Sarkaz do.
Only power...
Only power can let us survive!
The Sarkaz have never been interested in the resentment between you.
All I know is I've given you chances before. Multiple.
If you clean up your act and return to holing up in the north by the end of the day, I'm even willing to let all your petty acts here be water under the bridge.
Haha...
Bloody magnanimous of you. Should I be kneeling in thanks here, o kind 'General?'
You can dream on!
I've seen people like you... one too many times.
Let me guess, you like the idea... that we survive thanks to your charity?
I've had enough of it.
I've had enough of being a wager to you, a piece on the bloody board.
My fate, and the Tarans' fates... are ours to decide, no other way about it.
Yours to decide?
You think you've managed to decide anything? From the moment you came to Londinium, your fates were sealed.
You... shut well up! I've... I've had my fair share of failure, but...
So long as I kill you here, and take all the intel there is on Londinium, I can go back to The Leader head held high!
Kill me? With these grotesques of yours, barely able to lift themselves off the ground?
You know that even if the Gargoyles of the Royal Court stood here, they might not be victorious against me?
Don't you compare my Arts to your devil witchcraft—
Ten grotesques at once?
For not being an Infected Caster, you must have some natural gift.
Nevertheless, I don't even need to call upon the witchcraft you speak of to deal with these rugged little Arts animations.
Nngh... urgh!
I see your limits.
Have you noticed the split in your staff? Once your flashy little tool shatters, you'll find you suddenly have everything in common with the frail, ordinary Feline.

Ha... hahaha!
And what in the bloody hell...
Does that have to do with anything?
The stone staff emits a crack in response.
The ground beneath them trembles, the ruined grotesques all around standing up once again.

They stagger and charge at each other, binding to each other tight, and form into a singular great statue, close to five meters tall. With a great roar and a flap of its wings, it swoops in, bearing down on the Sarkaz.
Defend!
Yes, sir! General, you be on guard—
Take care of yourselves.
This counterfeit cannot harm me yet.
In testament to the Sarkaz's words, he uses no kind of Originium Arts.
The colossal grotesque turns to him with a howl, the wind from the beating of its wings blowing a torrent of bricks against him, its fangs almost touching his cheeks.
And yet he simply thrusts forward once.
The stone statue halts in midair, and seconds later, collapses into a sea of rubble.
There you are. No longer can you play about with those stones of yours.
Ha... devil...
Even now, you still act so... arrogant. All because of the damned... power... you were born with.
I'll kill you... I'll KILL YOU!
You think after killing me, you can scurry back to your Leader and reap your reward?
Even if you truly could pull it off... I invite you to wonder, would she welcome you back with open arms, or would she send you straight back to Londinium?
Relay to Count Mourning that The Leader assents to all handling of Dublinn forces within Londinium by the Sarkaz.
If necessary, apologize to that Sarkaz general—
—that Dublinn has no intent to ruin its peaceful relationship with the Regent of Kazdel.
It was never on the cards to work in true cooperation with him, given the Gaul at his side, but we're still in no rush to make ourselves enemies, are we?
As a token of goodwill, ask the Count to mediate, and have the other nobles' forces hand back the few Sarkaz Messengers they intercepted at the auxiliary plate close by.
Don't you worry. The Duke of Wellington will not forget the Count's efforts. The Leader and Dublinn, even less so.
Tell him he needn't panic. Once The Leader allays this current situation, we'll go meet up with him at Londinium.
...The Dublinn commander from before? You wonder how she's doing?
Mandragora... Mandragora.
I've been kind enough to her. I even went out of my way to plead The Leader for lenience. That's why Mandragora even went to Londinium. She barely scraped in that final chance.
You have to understand, The Leader's never been the least bit satisfied with what Mandragora and her cohort accomplished in County Hillock.
Why can't she just understand... that at this point, Dublinn no longer needs the title of the 'spectre force?'
Ultimately, we look to found a new nation for the Draco and Tarans—
And how can we hope to gain the masses' support, with nothing but wrath, animosity and their terror by intimidation alone?
