If she could just comprehend The Leader's intent, learn to stay the flames in her own heart, withdraw safe and sound from Londinium... At least, when The Leader reads what a good job she did, there'd still be a place for her.
You know as well as I do, The Leader's... never forgotten her first Taran compatriots. After all, it was together they came out from the ashes.
I might be meeting a Messenger from the outside, come daytime.
They'll surely be here on some count or baron's bidding, but without a doubt, behind them will be that duke from the Taran region.
It may be they'll thank me, for I rid them of an unstable lunatic commander in their place, and established a better outset for them in their coming negotiations.
Sarkaz are always seen as useful tools. Yes, on that point, we and you are alike, loathing these arrogant nobles.
However... you'll have to call it here, Mandragora.
A dagger? You would sneak up on me with a dagger?

Hahaha...
*cough* *cough* *cough* *cough*...
And suppose...
I can pull it off?
Do your nerves grow through your heart? Did I skewer your brain too, in the process?
I've told you, you can't kill me.
Moreover, even if you did, it would have no meaning at all.
No... meaning?
If... everything... has to have meaning, well... there never was meaning in people like us living, was there?
I'll kill you. Not because anyone ordered me to...
Just because...
It's what I want to do.
Truly... a lunatic.

A lunatic... me? You look at that face of yours, always so confident and cool...
Right, you'd be clueless...
Because you always get what you want done, so, so easy.
Once the one you most care about... dies by your side...
Once the one you most trust... casts you away...
Pins you to the ground with your own sword, even...
Maybe then you'll understand.
Is this your final defiance on the brink of death? These curses of yours can't sway me one iota.

Kheh... heh...
Then why don't you hurry up and kill me?
You don't even need me to do anything.
You're already dead, Taran.
Taran... You're calling me a Taran.
Sarkaz...
Laid on the ground, she raggedly forces a breath.
The floor is cooling, chilling, and she gradually loses sense of her own arms and legs. Were they broken at some point? Or have they numbed in fleeing for her life?
Do you want to live, Taran?
Who is it... calling to her?
The sewers are right beside you. Climb down there, and those disgusting nobles won't ever catch you again.
Yes, climb down.
The sewers give off the intense stench of rust and grease. Parts ripped from abandoned machines and cold scraps from a noble's dining table mix together.
She's starving, yet she revolts at this hunger.
Rust, grease, but she can smell another scent.
She knows it. The odor of death.
The sewers are a tomb for the insects living in nomadic cities.
The wealthy construct cemeteries inside the cities where land comes at a premium, and the commoners, with little to their name, have no choice but to cast ashes out beyond the city orbits.
And the insects? They die without a word in the cracks between the plates. They die where the sun will never shine upon them.
Do you dare walk to your death? Should you have the courage to die, that is all you need to be granted a new life.
Leader...
She just needs to crawl to her left, scant inches, and she'll clamber into the sewers... like back then.
And there on her right... just on her right.
Her Taran compatriot stares with eyes wide open, gazing silently upon her.
Sorry, Leader.
This time...
It's me... who doesn't need you.
She uses the last of her strength to push her right hand out. Her touch alights on her old friend, changed beyond recognition.
No one's... after us now. Let's go home.
Even though your meeting's end will be in death?
Someone stands before her. No firelight by his feet, pitch black reflected from his dagger.
Mandragora grins.
In her mind, she has met the true Death.
There's an entrance right below that abandoned factory. Push that crane out of the way and that'll do.
Alright, thank you for showing the way, Mr. Feist.
We'll be going along here. Everyone not wounded, please lend a hand to who are—
Bleeding hell. Doctor, we've got pursuers again!
How are they doing it?
Ever since leaving the kohl factory, we always get found no matter how many waves of enemies we repel.

opts: We can discuss that in a moment.
Let's handle these ones first.

lines.x: Right, this is our last chance to retreat underground.

We can't let the enemy follow us under at any cost. The Self-Salvation Corps has all their operations set up here, and the Sarkaz don't know which entrance is the correct one to these underground tunnels.
On the off-chance a Sarkaz finds this one, the Self-Salvation Corps' and the Sudean borough's efforts will all be laid waste to, just like that.

opts: Closure, ready the explosives.

lines.x: On it... uhh, what?
Doctor, you want me prepare to blow up this entranceway?
That definitely works as a form of insurance.
Are the Self-Salvation Corps gonna like that...?
Clovisia gave command authority to the Doctor. What the Doctor says, goes.
Amiya, we'll intercept the Sarkaz in the estate behind. Wait on my signal. If we fail to head them off, I want you to set off the bombs before it's too late.
They're over there!
Quit gawking around. Your opponent's right here.
Ugh––!
One team of ten Sarkaz. We can hold them down.
These few, but even once we do...
You... None of you are getting away.
The Sanguinarch can smell it... he'll find you one by one... tear your bodies open, cut your throats apart...
Who's this Sanguinarch?
Could it be... the one who just raided another unit of Self-Salvation Corps soldiers?
Raided? You Victorian Felines wouldn't be worth his... raiding.
He's simply... hunting prey. As the Prince of Blood's quarry, you wouldn't even register in his gaze.
No need to listen to their nasty comments.
Indra, do you remember that run-in with the Self-Salvation soldiers?
Burned into my eyes. What a bloody scene.
I've seen plenty of people die, but just smeared all over the ground like that... barely even looked human anymore.
Dagda, what are you doing? There's still some of 'em over there, we can't waste—
Siege, I beseech you. Let us get out of here.
Do you hear yourself? You're the one who wanted to fight the Sarkaz, now you're the one getting cold feet about it...
I've suffered the humiliation of turning tail once before. I can cope with it again.
So long as we can escort Siege safely out of this area, I'm willing to come back and wage war to the bloody end with these Sarkaz at any time.
Fine.
Siege, have you finally made your decision?
Perhaps I have.
Dagda, I've already seen your choice.
My... choice?
Go.
Vina?
Hold on...
Go and find the one you need.
Don't let these Sarkaz stand in your way.
Are all of us down now?
All surviving Self-Salvation soldiers are here.
It's all thanks to you that we could even make it here.

opts: What about Vina?
We're still missing four.

lines.x: We're here.
Three returning for the Siege Group.
Um, what about Miss Dagda?
Is... Is she alright?
Don't mention that bloody arsehole to me!
She's not going to feel it no matter how hard you pound the walls, love.
Er...
Dagda has her own things she needs to do. Amiya, I apologize to you and the Doctor for her departure.
No, no need to apologize. I sympathize, these are special circumstances. I can understand Miss Dagda's decision.
If we're sure we don't have to wait for her—

opts: Closure.

lines.x: Y—You got it!
That's that blown, then, eh...?
If Dagda wants to come back, maybe she'll... have issues finding the way...
She won't.
Vina, what's 'she won't?' She won't find her way to us, or she won't come back?!
Leave Vina alone. We've still got a ways forward to go.
Right. Forward we go. The Self-Salvation's soldiers are still waiting for us.
We're down thirteen civilians and nine soldiers.
Rhodes Island...
They're proving to be quite troublesome.
General, we've finished the head count on the remaining Dublinn soldiers.
Give them one chance.
Those willing to join us will enter the mercenary squad.
As for those who aren't... You already know what to do with them.
Yessir!
Not your best showing, General Manfred.
...I didn't expect for Confessarius's Messenger to still be here.
Both the Leader and the Regent are concerned about the battle situation unfolding in this inconspicuous corner.
I trust the Sanguinarch is no exception.
After all, his generosity last night took a lot of the pressure off of us.
Oh right, he wanted me to let you know, the atmosphere here is too filthy. He doesn't plan to wait around much longer.
At the moment, he is in the unfortunate process of requisitioning the property of a factory owner, and will be enjoying a late dinner.
Requisitioning?
I do hope that... whoever the Prince 'requisitioned' the factory from isn't one of our partners.
Does that matter?
These inefficient factories provide us with hardly anything worthwhile. All they do is breed pests.
His patience will only last until tomorrow night.
If you are unable to provide him with a satisfactory banquet, then he will 'sweep' these filthy streets as he sees appropriate.
Is this the Sanguinarch's intent, or the decision of the Military Commission?
Always fussing over these irrelevant details. This is exactly why you've been pinned down in this little dump. His Highness expected more of you.
...Please report back to the Prince: We are deeply grateful for his assistance, and will proceed with the next phase of the operation.
Very well. We look forward to seeing your results.
Heading off to war isn't nearly as much of a slog as talking to these Royal Court people, eh?
So this is where you've been hiding.
I assure you, I haven't been slacking off.
You can stop cradling that arm you nearly lost. Tell me, which of your old acquaintances did that to you? The one with the explosives, or the one with the sword?
Sorry, it hurts so bad I can't remember anything.
Since they left you with another arm to pick up a sword with, it seems whoever it was still has a soft spot for you.
It's almost daybreak.
...Yeah, looks like it.
Stop leaning against the wall in a daze. Break time's over.
Mercenaries aren't eligible for sick leave, right?
Let's go. This battle is far from over.
How many injured are there? Take them to get treated right now!
I'll also help out!
Mhm, thank you.
Bring Harmo over here. We have to speed up the evacuation.
Also, dispatch some Messengers outside the city. Worst case scenario, we need to be prepared to relocate the bulk of the population.
Yes, Commander!
Miss Siege...
Miss Siege?
Oh... Sorry, your voice always makes my mind wander off.
You must be exhausted. Just like our soldiers.
Thanks for your concern. I'm hanging in there.
The piece of intel you brought back— that the warriors of Team 6 were slaughtered by extremely terrifying Originium Arts, is critically important to us.
If only I could've gone to the surface to check on them sooner...
No, you would've been in danger too.
Besides, you've already done enough. If not for your quick-thinking, it might not have been just Team 6 that ran into him, but the rest of our troops and your people as well.
Who is he? Those Sarkaz soldiers seemed to call him a prince.
...Three months ago, a nobles' banquet was held in northern Londinium.

It began with great fanfare. The lights stayed on the entire night, and it wasn't until the servants went to clean the venue on the second day that they discovered that the only thing left was a still-playing record player.
Everyone was dead?
All killed in a single instant, it seems. Some of them still kept their posture from the moment of death, glasses raised in a toast.
Among the slain were those with connections with us. Heidi knew them too.
That's right.
I lost several old friends that night. The Self-Salvation Corps also lost a significant portion of its network throughout the city.
It's not for no reason that we retreated to the edge of the wall.
But it's thanks to Heidi's unfaltering efforts that we've managed to survive. And soon enough, we'll have a chance to face the Sarkaz head-on.
Before that though... we still need to escape the gaze of that terrifying individual.
Amiya, would you happen to know who he is?
If my hunch is correct, the person looming above us is probably... a vampire.
...This'll be tough.
How tough?
To be honest, it's probably easier for me to make a new one from scratch than try to glue the pieces together.
Don't have the parts.
Huh? No way...

We're in Londinium. Most of the city's industrial resources are controlled by the Sarkaz, and we don't exactly have the luxury of scrounging up scraps for something that won't be used on the battlefield.
Your work environment is way too...
Impoverished? You can speak your mind, the commander isn't here.
...Way too much of a challenge. *cough* *cough*...
Miss Closure...
What's up?
D-Did you know, when you talk about being challenged, your eyes seem to... light up? Physically.
Huh? Really? Must be because I'm getting excited... Is it scary?
I-It's fine.
To be honest, it wasn't until I met you that I found out... Uhh, that Sarkaz can do more than just fight.
Oh... Uh, there are a lot of people who aren't fighters, like me.
Naturally, I'm a vampire, so of course I was born with some handy talents.
Huh? Y-You... what is this speed?! It'd take a week for me to do what you did in a single night!
Why do you think the pillars of Rhodes Island are so good?
Take a good look at my eyes. If the Rhodes Island landship was filled with vampires, working at night would be no problem, and we'd cut down big on the electricity bill.
Are there... a lot of vampire engineers?
Of course not! They... They all...
Hey, why'd you start shuddering all of a sudden?
Blame Londinium. It's been a long time since I last felt the fear of being this close to a den of evil. Look, my hands are totally frozen stiff! And I need these hands!
Could it be... that Team 6 ran into...?
I sure hope not.
If so... For real, I need to give you guys a serious warning.
Just in case... I mean, just in case—if you ever run into another vampire, run!

No, it might already be too late by then.
I hope you'll never...
Never ever bump into them.
Morgan, you and Indra will go help the Self-Salvation Corps relocate.
Mm, I'll be there right away.

opts: Vina.

lines.x: What is it, Doctor?
Were you looking for me?

opts: I want to talk about Dagda.
How are you doing?

lines.x: Dagda... made her decision.
I... When I had to take up the mantle as their leader, I've never stopped thinking about how to do the right thing.
And at Rhodes Island, I saw how you went about doing that.
I'm fine... But, I'm really worried about Dagda.
It's no different from how you're worried about us, though. We both have our own responsibilities to shoulder.
Dagda is... pretty much in the same situation as me.
Every moment she stayed with me, her actions were bound by responsibility. No matter how many times I told her to make her own decisions, she would never be able to choose any other outcome.

When we truly have the capacity to make a choice, what truly matters is having the authority to make that so-called choice. Otherwise, it is nothing more than coercion, wrapped in a more palatable package.
I don't want to have to push her along ever again. She needs to accept herself, and then walk down her own path with conviction.

opts: What about your choice?
You can continue like this and become a regular soldier.
You can also go to them and tell them who you really are.
No matter what you decide, Rhodes Island and I will support you.

lines.x: Becoming a run-of-the-mill soldier might be the easy way out.
But my family name... No matter what you all decide to call me, I can't deny my name. It's a part of me.
I hope I didn't scare you by telling you the truth, Doctor.
Dr. Kal'tsit said she was willing to tell you, but I decided to do it myself.

opts: Your family name... is Victoria?

lines.x: You don't seem all that surprised.

opts: Maybe because I don't remember the past.
Maybe because I only care about the present.
Maybe because I'm looking ahead to the future.

lines.x: ...You don't remember the past?
Even though you've suffered so much due to that... I can't help but wonder, is your life a little bit easier because you can't remember?
That's right, what's most important is always the present.
When I made the decision to leave Victoria, all I wanted was for me and Indra to live to see this moment.
The future...
Which way... will my future take me?
Only I can answer that question. Nobody else can.
Phew... When I'm around you, I can always relax so easily.
It might be because... even when you found out the truth, the only person reflected in your eyes was the operator known as 'Siege'.
Instead of... Victoria.
Coming back to Londinium has brought back many memories.
But at the same time, so much has changed.
The decisions I make no longer solely affect my team.
Doctor... I don't actually want 'choice' to be an excuse that holds me back.

opts: I'm here today as a result of my choices.
My choices determine how I move forward.
Someone once said I'd find my own path.
Vina, I want to share those words with you too.
You always have the right to choose.

lines.x: ...Miss Heidi.
Amiya? I'm currently tending to the wounded.
You... practice medicine?
Only some basic nursing techniques.
This job inevitably brings me into emergency situations. To give me and my friends a better shot at survival, I had to at least learn to use a first-aid kit.
You're really amazing.
All we're doing is protecting our home. But Kal'tsit... and you, Amiya, you all carry so much more responsibility on your shoulders.
...We've lost a lot of people on this operation.
I... I feel the pain of the Self-Salvation Corps' soldiers. They've never had to deal with such horrific enemies before.
The Sanguinarch... Did he come here because of us?
Amiya, what you need to hear isn't me saying 'no, it's not your fault.'
Yes...
Clovisia said she was afraid Rhodes Island would get the Self-Salvation Corps involved in a war they weren't a part of.
If working with us puts them at greater risk...
During last night's operation, what would've happened if Rhodes Island had not been there?
Without W colluding, had she not stopped the mercenary leader... and without Rhodes Island's operators keeping Manfred at bay, we wouldn't even be having this conversation right now.
The calculus of exchanging one set of lives for another simply does not exist for Rhodes Island—
But even if Kal'tsit were here, I'm sure she would say every opportunity comes with its risks.
I understand.
That's why we have to be better prepared.
Miss Heidi, I need more information from within the city. Not counting the Sanguinarch, how many kings do the Sarkaz have inside Londinium?
Excuse me, I'd like a bag of bread.
I don't need your money, lass. Take all of these with you.
Huh?
Hurry along, and don't come out here again.
What do you mean...?
I know who you are. Actually, we can all tell.
Even if you took off your equipment and changed your clothes, both your actions and the look in your eyes won't fool anyone.
...Thank you.
I might as well pack up too. Don't think there's business to be had today.
We're doing another sweep of this street! Take away anyone whose name is on the list!
Wh-What's going on? I... I'm just a restauranteur. Didn't you come by to have fried finmeat before...?
You're the one we're after.
Your cover runs pretty deep. Selling fried meat to us by day, peddling information to the rebels by night?
Take them away! And check those buildings next door. They're all rebels!
Why are the Sarkaz suddenly going around grabbing people?
H-Hurry, take a turn at the alley just ahead and find the others—
Ugh—!
Another one down.
You lot, search that alley over there. Don't let a single one of them escape this borough.
Report, we ran into Sarkaz as soon as we came out...
No, they weren't guarding the city exits, they suddenly appeared in the middle of the streets!
Commander, do you think our comms have been compromised?
Please be careful... The soldiers dispatched out of the city have all perished. I can't hide for much longer either!
I—
Bloody hell, why can't I shake them off?
The Sarkaz seem to have suddenly figured out all our passages and networks!
In just... a single night...?
What exactly happened last night?
Haha, wouldn't you like to know.
Huh? What the hell are you? My blade snapped clean in half—
The way you're dressed... Are you a Rhodes Island operator? Thank you. You guys saved us again.
Hurry back.
Wait, be sure to tell Amiya and the Doctor that the underground isn't safe anymore.
The traps we ran into yesterday... aren't the only ones we've discovered.
Have you finished cleaning up the streets?
Yes, General. Everyone on the list has already been dealt with.
Some tried to flee to other boroughs or leave the city just as you said, and we stopped them as well.
Only a few slipped through our net... The soldiers sent to chase after them seemingly ran into the same Sarkaz from Rhodes Island as yesterday.
Let them squirm their way underground.
That'll make it easier for us to flush them all out at once.
...Multiple of our contact points above ground were attacked all at once?
What of our Messengers? They got taken as well?
Doctor, I want to hear your view on this.

opts: Rhodes Island wasn't told where these points were.

lines.x: The only ones with access to that information are our own troops.

opts: We were tailed on the way back yesterday.

lines.x: It seems the seeds of crisis were sown last night.
But I don't want to doubt our own soldiers.
There's no way they'd betray their brothers and sisters.

opts: The commander is responsible for the team.
We can't overlook any hypothetical.
How many soldiers were rescued?

lines.x: Twelve people came back with Heidi.
Among them, nine only ever provided us information on occasion.
The other three helped us transport supplies, so it is possible they know the locations of some contact points.

opts: How many more are in Sarkaz hands?

lines.x: ...A lot more.
Doctor, you know better than anyone how difficult last night's operation was. As much as I'd like to rescue everyone, I'm forced to admit that we're not yet strong enough.
Heidi's done good work. Being able to save all those people already went above and beyond my expectations.

opts: The Sarkaz couldn't know who would be rescued.
Unless everyone defected.
You don't place a pawn that won't be used.

lines.x: There were a lot of uncertainties surrounding our operation last night...
I doubt the Sarkaz could have full control over everything.
That's simply not possible.
That's right.
The Sarkaz commander isn't a fool. He would have found a way to make sure his plan succeeded.
So, if there really were traitors among the soldiers we saved...
In all likelihood, they would've been the easiest for us to rescue.

opts: That narrows it down considerably.
The most obvious suspects are clear.

lines.x: Still, I don't want to fling these accusations around about soldiers willing to bleed and die for their homeland.
We need proof.
How do you suppose we get that, Doctor?

opts: Please call Feist and Rockrock.
Let's go visit the wounded together.

lines.x: Why aren't you moving? The commander just sent news that Bill's about to wake up.
...Rockrock.
I want to tell her that I'm stepping down as captain of the operation team.
Take a look at these.
Packages? What's inside them?
Johnny and Gabby's belongings. I picked out the stuff to send back to their families.

What remains are their tools, weapons, and equipment. If you really... can't get them out of your head, then pick out something useful and polish it up a bit. That way they'll at least still be with us on the battlefield.
Hah...
Sounds like the vice-captain rejected my resignation... Let's go see Bill then.
You're awake?
Yeah.
Something came up, so Miss Heidi had to leave. She was originally the one in charge of coordinating the treatment of the wounded here.
Wait, you're from Mr. Feist's team. Bill, right?
Mr. Bill, should I go call Feist and Rockrock over?
Ah, no, that's alright.
Miss Amiya, I decided to get up to talk to you.
...Huh?
Well, uh... I've always been curious about you.
Amiya of Rhodes Island...
Why do they trust you so much? You're just a Cautus in her teens.
Are you... talking about our operators?
We've been through so much together. You're correct that they trust me, but that trust is mutual.
As far as I can tell, it's no different for everyone in the Self-Salvation Corps either. You also trust Feist, Rockrock, and Clovisia, right?
Haha... Mutual trust, huh? That might be a part of it. After all, you've been through your share of trials and tribulations.
But your authority does not come from your bloodline, nor have you taken your position by force. You are far too young to have earned the amount of trust you enjoy.
...Mr. Bill?

Deep down, you've thought about these things too, haven't you? But you don't dare show it. Your fears, your worries, your anxiety... Everything is suppressed by the responsibilities they've forced onto you.
Are you worried about me? Did you overhear my conversation with Miss Heidi?
Mm... Maybe. I heard everything.
I appreciate the concern, but my responsibilities were not forced onto me.
Our operators placed their faith in me, and I am not going to let them down.
Of course... Of course. Since the very beginning, they've always been devoted to you.
But Amiya... When they look at you, how do you know that they're not looking right past you? Searching for another soul that has endured the twists and turns of fate?
Everything you believe, do you truly believe it, or...
Are you dreaming a dream given to you by somebody else... one that you can't wake up from no matter how much you struggle?
Mr. Bill... No, that's not who you are!
Who exactly are you?
Allow me to return the question.
Amiya... Who are you?

lines.st: Amiya, do you like the stories I tell? You'll still be able to sleep well, won't you? I don't want you to have nightmares.
You won't? Oh, what a sweet child you are.
That's right, while we may look different in many ways, we are both children given life and nurtured by this great land.
Some of the stories you've heard are legends that others find terrifying, but they are indeed the history of the Sarkaz.

lines.x: You shouldn't be here.
I see the energy gathering inside your palm. Have you not figured out who I am? Why aren't you attacking?
I cannot touch your emotions, 'Mr. Bill'.
But... I also cannot feel any malice.
Oh?
You could have continued pretending to be asleep, yet you said all those things to me...
Hah, didn't I already tell you? We are very curious about you.
If not for you, we'd have no desire to come here either. Theresis has no sway over us.
No matter what you're after... this is far too cruel.
Cruel...?
Do you mean this face we're borrowing?
Yes, which is why I need you to leave.
Black lines surged from her palm once more, weaving a net around the man in the Self-Salvation Corps uniform.
One strand condensed into a lightless blade, pressed against his throat.
What a... remarkably gentle threat.
Amiya, a lot of people have told you this before, yes?
You really are just like her.
Not another word out of you, please.
Unfortunately, you're still too immature.
Always be aware of your surroundings before you make a move.
...Miss Amiya?!
You, you... you're trying to kill Bill?
No, I...
Doctor, I take it that you didn't ask me to bring everyone here just to see this scene?

opts: I wanted to see who'd make the first move.
You're already looking at the 'traitor'.

lines.x: I assume you're not referring to Amiya. Then, Bill?
Rockrock... Rockrock, help me...
I'd rather die myself... than betray you!
Stay away from him. I... No, nobody knows the full extent of his Originium Arts.
His words are, or rather his very existence is... dangerous.
Miss Amiya... Can I trust you?
Last night, we fought side-by-side. I thought Rhodes Island truly had our backs, just as you said.
But...
Your expression scares me. It's as if I've seen it somewhere before...
That's right, you're already mourning the pain and suffering you're about to cause.
If so, then... why? Why go through with it?!
She... The Sarkaz took my father from me, right before my eyes. Are you going to take my family away too?
I'm sorry, Miss Rockrock... But it's too late. Your family member's already gone.
But, Bill's right in front of us!
This must be difficult... for you to accept.
...I don't understand.
Look, Amiya, it's not that we're not willing to believe you, but we don't have your Arts. We can't see what you see.
Commander... I can see it.
Captain?
He might not actually be the same Bill we know.
But those wounds on his body... he got all those from saving me yesterday!
You said it yourself, Rockrock. The Sarkaz are masters of disguise, but we can't let ourselves be fooled.
But how...?
Look, I brought along the Metal Crab I was going to give to Bill.
See for yourself.
Ugh, when did this thing crawl onto me?
He didn't even spare it a glance. The only thing in his eyes is Miss Amiya. He's so proud of having deceived us that he's long forgotten... about the bond between us.
All because of this insignificant little machine?
Don't you think you're being a bit too hasty, Feist? Aren't we sworn blood brothers?
I've made a lot of hasty decisions these last few days.
Bill, my good man... if he were still around, he'd be ruthlessly making fun of me. I miss him. I miss him so much.
Whoever you are... Don't make me pry that mask off your face with my wrench.
Rockrock, are you going to break my heart, just like him?
I'll never turn my back on my family.
But you... you're acting less and less like him.
He would never talk like that. He would never try to manipulate us.
...So you really don't think I'm the real Bill? Feist, are you really okay with that?
I'll have you know, if so, then that means your friend died in vain.
Anyone else may have the right to say that, but you sure as hell don't!
Haha. You're pretty smart, but unfortunately for you...
It's too late.
Artilleryman, are you ready?
Yes, General. The cannon is aimed at the target.
Remember, we're leveling every single building except the train station and the tracks.
Yessir!
Increase the output to maximum.
M-Maximum? That might blow right through to the structural levels—
Did you hear what the Confessarius's Messenger said? In their eyes, this whole tract of land, including the factories above, is worth nothing to us.
Then... Then, what about our people underneath...?
Hoederer will guard the safe areas under the city walls.
But what about—
Uhh... Who do you think is heading underground to deal with the rebels?
The Sanguinarch?
As long as you get it. If these toys can take HIM out, I'd be... extremely surprised.
As for the other one...
I've been curious for a while now. If it came to an all-out battle with the Prince, who would come out on top?
Thank goodness you're all here—
Huh? Why does it feel like I walked right into a fight about to break out?
Anyway Doctor, I sent up some drones like you requested— They're really about to resume! Th-They're pointing those cannons right at us!
Notify Heidi and Harmo. We're evacuating immediately.
Hold on, I'm getting a strong Originium reading! They're aiming at our current location!
We've only got ten seconds!
Nine—

opts: Spare us the countdown.
Run, now!

lines.x: —Why are you holding on to me?
You said it yourself, we're sworn blood brothers. I don't want to... pass judgment too hastily.
Captain... I don't think... I can carry the two of you...
I'll admit that you people are very... interesting.
Goodbye, Captain.
Goodbye, Rockrock.
Bill wanted you to live on. Those were his last words.
What's the situation?
Areas D and E have been severely damaged, and the structures in other areas have sustained various degrees of damage as well!
Nineteen people have light injuries, with nobody seriously injured as of yet.
Fortunately, we followed the instructions given by you and the Doctor, and managed to move most of them to a safe area ahead of time––
No, nowhere's safe right now.
Everywhere around you is dangerous... Our entire stronghold has been completely exposed to the Sarkaz.
They're not going to stop until they've blasted this place into smithereens to make sure we have nowhere to hide.
How much time do we have?
Thirty minutes. Uhh, maybe even less than that.
The nearby plates are pretty old, and if we take the power of the artillery we've seen before and multiply that by the theoretical maximum output value––
We won't even have thirty minutes before the structure above us collapses right onto our heads!
Let Harmo know and we'll start the train right away.
Train?
You all should've seen it before. A few kilometers above us is a train station.
I thought all the stations were occupied by the Sarkaz.
Then all we have to do is take that one back.
Now that the Sarkaz have cut off all our pathways to other boroughs, this is the last chance we have to evacuate Sudean.
Heidi, once we leave Sudean, do you have a way to keep us out of sight?
Yes. I've been preparing for this.
Once we arrive at the next station, we'll have friends who will disguise us until we successfully enter Central Londinium.
Good.
The biggest question mark will be whether or not we can safely board the train––
Clovisia, how far are we from the city walls?
Very close, if we take the underground passages.
I'd like to try climbing them from beneath.
Amiya, are you planning to...?
We'll buy time for the Self-Salvation Corps to retreat.
Block all exits from the targeted plate and keep an eye on the train station. I don't want them crawling into the next gutter over.
Set up the motion-tracking ground targeting system. In case they still have some fight left in them and plan to escape through the surface via vehicle, we'll greet them with even more intense fire.
Strengthen the defenses of all points inside the city walls.
Also, change the control passwords for the defense artillery so no one but me can deactivate it.
Good, you're taking adequate precautions.
General?! You're here...?
In a matter of moments, these pesky insects have nowhere to hide. The Londinium rebels will never again dare to poke their heads out.
More importantly, nobody will be able to go in and out of the dark passages we cannot see. The only thing the dukes outside will hear will be our hand-picked messages.
No one will interfere with our project's progress again.
Manfred, am I not welcome to share this moment of triumph with you?
Please do not mock me so, Your Highness.
Oh? You've gotten better than before. We hardly spoke five sentences, and you've already realized we are not Theresis.
Your Highness... Whether or not His Majesty is willing to turn a blind eye to you, should you draw the attention of the Confessarii, they will surely petition His Majesty to restrict your movements.
Very well. As you said, the Military Commission cannot allow two men to call the shots––
In that case, I'll shift to an appearance I'm more comfortable with.
How unusual that you're willing to meet with me using the same appearance you take at the Royal Court.
We vowed not to use the faces of the dead, so as to not scare you and your men.
You came back sooner than expected.
Even though you helped us root out the rebels' den, they may still have an escape route I haven't found.
If you could uphold your end of the deal and follow them until every last rebel has fallen into our hands...
Do you really think that Doctor can keep us hidden for so long?
More importantly, Manfred, you must not forget that we do not belong to you.
I was out of line. Please forgive me.
Don't talk to us the same way you do to old pink-eyes. We've told you before, we won't fall for that drivel.
As you wish, Your Highness.
Since you decided to come back ahead of time, did you find what you were looking for underground?
Hmm... That 'King of Sarkaz' is stronger than we thought, but is far from mature.
She knows little of what she is about to face. If we want to learn more about her, that will have to wait until she meets Her Majesty.
You'd better not let the Confessarii hear those words.
And why not?
They'll think the Damazti Cluster of the Royal Court... had a change of heart.
And why would you think that matters to us?
Manfred, we've already done what we promised you. You and the vampire can clean up the rest of the mess yourselves.
Oh, right... Next time we speak, pick an identity for us that is still alive.
Vina, Amiya and the Doctor have already left.
Good.
The first wave of Self-Salvation Corps forces are rushing towards the station above us.
Hurry, pick up the pace!
Once they capture the station, injured troops and non-combatant civilians will be the first to board the train.
Mr. Balin, can I trouble you and the medics to tend to the injured with restricted mobility? Everyone, please pay attention to the signal lights on both sides of the passage, and don't get lost!
Most of the command structure will go with the injured and the civilians, except––
Have all our people entered the retreat channel?
The integrity of the superstructure is continuing to erode! Commander, we have to get out of here quick!
As long as everyone's inside, I'll be right behind you!
12th, sitrep!
Commander, the underground areas won't hold for much longer. Are enemies really still coming down?
Report!
Vina, should we go with Balin's group?
We're staying.
Staying? You're going to stay here with Commander Clovisia?
I have a feeling... Maybe she's getting it too.
The strongest enemy... might not be above us right now.
The cannons.
Horn, the defensive cannons are activating again! This time, they're targeting the plate closest to the city walls. From what I can tell, they're not aiming at us...
But there's no guarantee that the surrounding areas are safe.
Run, hurry––
Where's my daughter? Has anyone seen my daughter!
Look out!
Get inside the sturdiest building as quickly as you can! ––No, get away from the city walls, now!
O-Okay...
So this... is Londinium's defensive artillery.
Based on the current situation, it won't be long until the cannon fire penetrates the plate and destroys the base structure.
Those damned Sarkaz... Is this the moment they've been waiting for? Are they trying to destroy the city?
No. They've only activated a small number of cannons, and their targets are quite clear.
They're trying to eradicate us... and our friends.
Horn, what should we do?
Among the soldiers who came back with us yesterday, how many of them were originally part of the city's defense forces garrisoned here?
Call them over. Bring the best equipment we've got.
Tell them–– it's time to take back these walls from the Sarkaz.
We're almost there.
Careful, don't trip and fall––
Are you hanging in there?
*sigh*... It's hard to say.
Yeah, I know where you're coming from.
I've also made the big mistake of trusting people I shouldn't.
After that, for an entire month, it took everything I had to force myself to keep going.
As soon as I stopped, I... Well, I'd feel like I couldn't look Amiya in the eye.
Well, the battle's not over yet.
People don't have enough time to catch their breaths. It'd be too much of a luxury for me to mourn or wallow in self-doubt.
Mr. Feist, do you need help? I can calm you down.
How? Is that some sort of Originium Arts?
Something like that.
So... do you also help your men with psychological treatment, under normal circumstances?
If the operator thinks it's necessary.
Ugh...

opts: Relax. Amiya's looking out for all of you.
Amiya won't see our people become cold war machines.

lines.x: Mr. Feist, did I come off the wrong way?
No, no, that's not it. I was just thinking you have some truly amazing Arts.
Even in a big place like Londinium, I've hardly seen anything like them before.
Miss Amiya, I appreciate your kindness. But, I don't think I'm ready to drive that scene out of my head so quickly.
Then we'll just have to get right back to work.
Let's do it.
The drones are showing that the defense structure layer above us is... very thick.
Can it be blown up?
Not likely. If Londinium's defenses could get blown up so easily... Well, let's just say, we would've stormed the Shard yesterday.

opts: You'll find a way.
I believe in you, Closure.

lines.x: I appreciate that, Doctor. You sure know how to twist your employees' arms.
Hmm... I've finished scanning the lift, and as long as you guys can open up enough of a gap for me to push the drone through, I should be able to hack into the lift and take control of it.
Of course, you'll have to find a bigger gap to get yourselves up first.
Doctor, don't you think you should send a signal and call Misery over?

opts: He has work to do.

lines.x: Fine, I guess we'll have to do it ourselves.
Maybe the locals over here can give me some inspiration.
If you can locate the support structure and apply an extremely strong external force, you might be able to open a little seam...
Someone's rapidly approaching our location!
Did the Sarkaz expect us to find a way to climb up the wall?
Who cares about that? I'll have the drone pack some Originium explosives!
Feist, calculate the position. I'll think of some way to amplify the explosive effect––
He's here!
....Hoederer.
We meet again, leader of Rhodes Island.
What happened to W...?
Are you sure you should be worrying about her right now? I heard you folks were short on time.
I'm willing to set aside whatever happened between you two. As well as... what happened at Chernobog, with Scout...
But, Mr. Mercenary, if you intend on standing in our way, I'll cut you down without mercy.
For any mercenary, dying at the hands of Her Majesty's heir would be a great honor.
You'd better hurry up, Amiya!
The bomb I planted just started ticking down––
One minute! That's all the time we've got!
I'll stop Hoederer...
No... Miss Amiya, let me handle this mercenary.
Mr. Feist?
I've found the support column. Plus, I have a plan, though I don't know how solid it is... If it works out, you and the Doctor need to seize the opportunity to get up the wall.
I know you, young warrior.
I also know that look in your eyes. The weight of everything you've lost has finally begun to set in, and you've lost your ability to trust in the soundness of your own decisions.
Lost?
Sorry, but even if I had the time, I'm not interested in a heart-to-heart with a butcher.
Over here––!
Another smoke bomb?
Even if I'm short a usable arm, your little tricks won't work on me.
Come on, Sarkaz. Give me your worst!
Let me taste your sword, your machete, whatever weapons you've got on you––
Trying to draw me away? Not a very sensible strategy.
Empty bravado with no self-awareness isn't going to buy you any miracles.
Even if all your men were here, you wouldn't stand a chance against me.
Ugh...
Pretty ugly run there. Without help, you're going to bleed out soon enough.
After all, you're just a craftsman... You and your men wave around wrenches and hammers, and hope that is enough to hold back Sarkaz blades tempered by centuries of war?
Beg the King of Sarkaz for help. Have her deal with me.
The King of Sarkaz?
What... are you talking about...?
Sarkaz... You're the ones who brought war to Londinium.
This is a war between us!
Thirty seconds!

opts: Amiya, focus your energy.

lines.x: Hold on tight, Doctor!
Mr. Feist, I need your location––
Mercenary... Are you seeing this?
Another flashy toy? Stop resisting. You can't fool me.
Sure, you're right––
Please, one last push!
A metal crab? A mechanical toy? What's your play here? Quit screwing around.
No, that's the most important thing you've taken from me.
I've never fought alone...
Look, Old Bill helped me out again. It's all thanks to your paranoia, mercenary.
My paranoia? You...
You're using my––!
Ha... Haha! Do you finally get it now?
Every time you swung your blade at me... you were carving your path to defeat!
Amiya, Hoederer cut through the structural layer!
Hurry, take aim! The bomb's about to blow!
Three... Two... One––
Hrrgh––
It's been split open! That's a big enough gap! Alright, you're up, drone––
The lift is ours now!
Doctor, let's head up!
Feist, are you hanging in there? Grab onto the zipline, and we'll head up after them––
You're not going anywhere!
Closure, have any more explosives?
Yeah, catch!
You...
Thanks for the hand, mercenary.
Do we... Do we need to keep increasing our reload rate?
Are you seriously trying to eradicate every mobile plate in the city?
Got a problem with the general's orders?
I... I'm just concerned that bombardment on this scale will have an effect on the walls...
Nothing like this has ever happened before. If Londinium wants to swap out plates inside the city, we'd simultaneously have to shift around the walls...
Didn't ask.
Are you going to obey, or are you going to die? Those are your only two options.
What's the matter?
I think he still has a third option.
You––! Urgh...
Can you still stand, Captain?
I... I'll be alright. Who are you?
An old battle buddy.
Over here! Someone snuck in!
This shield? Where the hell did Victorian soldiers come from––
It feels pretty nice to use a weapon I'm familiar with.
Captain, can this model of cannon... take out the control system of the city defense artillery?
No... It's not possible...
I was expecting as much.
Horn, we've mopped up the Sarkaz outside!
Make sure to thank Mr. Misery again on my behalf.
Captain, is there any other way to deactivate the artillery?
No need for the long face. You've been praying that we'd come.
After all, you didn't even cover up your old ID from the city defense forces.
This is just a blunder. The Sarkaz... won't kill me over something like this yet.
But if you help us, you'll be put to death?
And my family too.
They'll get dragged in. The Sarkaz can kill them off at any time.
I understand.
Carle, put your sights on this traitor.
Um, Lieutenant... Where am I supposed to aim...?
Here. At my heart.
If I die... Perhaps only then, the Sarkaz will forget about me, and stop haunting my family.
If you've made up your mind, we'll work with you.
Alright. Thank you, Lieutenant.
If you want to turn off the cannons, there are only two ways.
One, is to have the Sarkaz general stop them himself... The other, is to completely destroy the control room.
For the first method to work, we'll have to defeat Manfred.
That's right. As for the second option... We don't have enough firepower. And besides... Are you prepared to tear down a cornerstone of Londinium's walls with your own hands?
I know it's what we should do.
What about you, Captain? Are you prepared?
I've been waiting for this moment... for a long time.
Lieutenant, you missed.
Bloody hell, I suppose my hand slipped.
Help him down the lift. Whether he lives or dies... should be in his own hands.
Too much has happened on these walls today. We don't need to add another traitor.
What about us? Should we head up as well?
Let's go. We're going to pay Manfred another visit.
Woah, this lift is going up way too fast––
L-Let's not talk right now... or I might vomit all over the Doctor.
After all, we've been dragged several hundred meters via zipline...

opts: Don't do that again.
You'll get used to it.

lines.x: Everyone catch your bearings? Enemies are approaching!
We've got more intruders here! Report to the general immediately!

opts: Amiya, intercept the attack!
We'll catch up to the runner.

lines.x: Closure, we need to find some way of shutting down those cannons––
If that means we have to take out Manfred, we'd better make it quick.
The longer we take, the more the remaining Self-Salvation Corps on the ground will be at risk.
*cough* *cough*...... *cough* *cough* *cough*...
To think you'd get blasted like this by a boy who's barely seen a fight... What an embarrassment you are.
Even a tiny metal crab, when backed into a corner... will pinch your hand.
You'd better hope Manfred buys your story.
You shouldn't be here. It's too risky.
So you'd rather lean against rubble gasping for breath than let me lend you a hand?
*wheeze*... If you wanted to hold me up... the other side would be better.
Thanks to her, I can't move this arm anymore.
You ran into her. What did you think?
Well, she noticed 'Her Highness.'
Then, she...
Didn't change at all.
You're positive? We all saw what she was like back then.
Even when she was chasing down those assassins, her body riddled with wounds, could she have imagined... that Her Majesty wasn't dead?
She's not the volatile, impulsive mercenary she used to be.
So, she's getting even crazier?
Who can say?
The truth is in front of us. She snuck into Londinium by herself and didn't toss a single bomb for two weeks. Not even Manfred noticed her.
The way she was before, she would've rushed straight into Westhaleg and buried a hundred landmines under Theresis's throne before getting herself killed by Confessarii.
I can reasonably assume she's formed some wiser plans with those folks from Babel... Or rather, Rhodes Island.
You almost traded your life away just to make sure of that? Aren't you playing it a bit too fast-and-loose?
Manfred's underlings always have eyes on me. This was the only safe way we could meet.
Too bad for Schwab...
He made his peace the moment he sided with W.
Or should I say... We've all made our peace.
I sure hope so.
Who's there?
I smell... Confessarius!
I told Manfred you can't be trusted.
Hoederer, did you really think you could hide your clever little gestures from our eyes?
This is all a misunderstanding.
Oh? Unfortunately for you, you won't have another chance to twist words around in front of the leader and the Regent.
You don't need to pretend to bow your head anymore. Pick up your sword and revel in your last battle, mercenary.
If that... is an order, then...
You are powerless.
You can't hope to protect your companions.
Not a single shadow here... can escape my eyes.
Hoederer...
Run!
No, you're not giving the orders here. It cost us a lot to come this far––
Even more reason why we can't both die here!
Worry not. I'll execute both of you here, together.
You may take a moment to decide who goes first––
Nngh?!
He went down... without a struggle?
You... It's you.
He got too cocky.
No one can claim to master all shadows. Infinite possibilities lie where the eyes cannot perceive.
*cough*... *cough* *cough* *cough*...
Hurry, get out of here. If you want to live, return to the sunlight.
What about you?
I still have other things to do.
I'll have you know... Confessarii underlings aren't the only ones here.
What's that sound?
Commander, the metal structure behind us just fell––
That's not all...
He's here.
You feel it too?
Vina, I didn't hear anything except the sound of shelling.
The sound of his heartbeat disappeared.
What?
Run!!!
We've lost contact with the 12th––
They've all been sacrificed.
Yes...
Their voices completely vanished.
These... pitch-black pipes should amplify any and all sounds.
Breathing or footsteps, the clacking of a metal crab crawling through the pipe, or the rumble of machinery––
These pipes will record the vibrations of any signs of life.
And in an instant... all of those voices were devoured.
You mean to say, this darkness behind us––
Is all the life it has devoured.
Just like what happened at that banquet three months ago.
Engineering team, close the first safety lock!
Yes, Commander!
Miss Siege, you've... got sharp senses.
Are you familiar with Londinium's underground structure?
I remember Feist saying... you're from around these parts?
I've... only been here once.
But I've heard a lot of stories about these pipes.
A hundred years ago, a terrible fire ripped through the heart of Londinium. Only the royal family and a few nobles managed to escape the flames.
After that, a legend began to circulate that a magic circle was hidden beneath the palace that could allow the royal family to escape any disaster.
When I first heard that story, I couldn't help but wonder... would our royal family also use these very same pipes for their escape?
Hmm... The story I heard was very similar to your version.
The workers who built Londinium, the posh aristocrats and nobles, and the royal family itself–– are all forced to take the same route during times of crises.
That already is a story that writes itself.
Commander, the safety lock is starting to give!
This door cannot hold him back.
Are you serious? This door's thickness can withstand seven or eight salvos from an armored vehicle!
Retreat! Fall back!
With a sharp, earsplitting noise, a fissure appears in the middle of the metal door.

Under the tearing and folding of the tremendous force behind the door, the thick metal sheet seems thinner than paper. The fissure continues to grow, the dark void behind it like a monstrous, abyssal maw.
Close the second safety lock!
Understoo–– Arrrgh!!
The door slams shut with a heavy thud, but the person sealing the door suddenly slips. Before he has a chance to scream, something invisible drags him into the shadows behind the door.
A smudge of blood seeps through the door's crack.
But not even the pool of blood is allowed to escape. In an instant, the shadows lurch through the door and instantly devour half the passageway.
Another massive roar rings out, though it is unclear whether it came from overhead or behind.
The pipe shudders, and the shadow's edge blurs like a predator's mouthparts, extending to latch onto its prey's ankle.
I... I've also heard of another story.
During a battle two hundred years ago, the Victorian Army joined forces with two other nations to lay siege to a city called Kazdel.
One night, an entire army of heavily-armed troops disappeared in the valley where they were stationed.
The count who had dispatched the army sent men to investigate their whereabouts, but only saw the crimson of the setting sun reflected against the mountain walls.
And as everyone remembered, that day... the sun had not come out at all.
The third safety lock––
Is this... the last one?
Clovisia doesn't answer her.
Just like her answer... Devoured by the monster flooding through the pipes.
So you're here.
...Manfred?
It seems the Sanguinarch will be greatly disappointed. He detests dark, damp places the most, yet went into the rebels' subterranean nest to seize you personally.
He must surely have caught up to your friends by now. One wonders if the cries from underground will carry so far up this tower—
You wicked... wicked...
Wicked devil?
No, I don't admire his methods whatsoever. The rebels who died to my cannon fire are far luckier than the ones suffering his torment.
You're both just the same!
In your eyes, maybe so. Whenever you use 'devil' and such to refer to us all, it shows that fear and hatred have long clouded your eyes.
The Victorians regard the Sarkaz as a collective of barbarians reveling in slaughter.
Those who thought like that are now either kneeling before the throne, or are now pale skeletons dried upon the city walls.
You, and Theresis... the army you've led has devastated this city.
No matter how you claim you and the vampire are different, you're both invaders here, hands dripping with blood.
You seem to misunderstand, Cautus of Rhodes Island.
I'm not solely drawing lines between myself and the Prince of Blood—yes, in a certain respect, his predilections disgust me.
But I, like him, do not work the way all the kings of the Court do.
When they stand in front of you, they see Her Majesty's successor, and they may treat you with skepticism, curiosity or disgust for it.
You're...
I can only feel vigilance from you.
Yes, Cautus. I simply see you—as a dangerous enemy.
—Did he just toss a grenade at us?
Hold onto the wall!
It's his Arts... he's using Arts energy to make shockwaves, he's trying to throw us off the city!
Ugh... can't stand straight—it's like every part of the ground's erupting...
The drones can't get near him! Any closer at all and they lose balance—
Everyone... get behind me!
Hrrgh––
A—Amiya, are his Arts fusing with yours?
He's deliberately narrowed and sharpened his attack. He's breaking down the energy layers I form...
What?! You were able to resist a Draco's flames, though!

opts: He's familiar with our combat style.
His Arts are targeted at Amiya.

lines.x: I knew full well you'd come. You think I'd just sit here waiting for you?
There—There goes another one! It's not just the ground, it's, it's above us, wherever red flashes by, it explodes in midair—
Miss Amiya, can't... can't you use mental Arts?
Can you try and just cram his head with some kind of rubbish?
No.
He's too well-defended, and he's too calm. I can feel the edges of his emotions, but... but right now, his heart only thirsts for victory.

opts: He's stalling for time.

lines.x: Yes... his mind isn't set on this battle.
He's just waiting for the vampire and defense artillery to destroy the resistance!
Stalling for time? Decent conjecture.
Doctor, I've always been more intrigued by you than that Cautus who lucked into the power of the King.
You've faced graver circumstances than this on the battlefield—the choices you make always seem to be the ones more beneficial to the whole.
Please, tell me, what are you about to do next?
Will you expend all your efforts here to kill me and eliminate a long-term threat, or think of some other way to get around me and save those Victorians below?
Uwaaagh!
Mr. Balin, what's happening up ahead?
Partial collapse in the passageway! Three fighters couldn't escape in time—
Route around it! We're pressing on.
Remember, no matter who we lose on this journey, the rest of us cannot stop.
Up, quick, do you see that train?
The Sarkaz haven't found us yet, from the looks of it. Let's think of a way to blend in there, and grab the controls—
Harmo, watch out!!
Sarkaz Crossbowman! They've ambushed us!
Rockrock, one step out and we'll get hit! Best we run back underground!
Going back's... riskier still.
The Commander says our only chance is to get the train moving before the Sarkaz destroy the space underground.
But Harmo...
Captain Harmo gave his life.
I'm in charge now.
Listen. We have to start the train immediately after we interrupt their shelling.
If I fall, then it's down to the next one—even if just one man's left in the end, we have to get him out of here!
I think this door's almost a lost cause, isn't it?
Commander, we'll hold them off!
Don't. You can't.
Ha, ha... you think we don't know that, Rhodes Islander?
Men of the 13th, with me!
Hey, you're running backwards! Don't be daft—
The—The door's down!
Shoot, shoot! Blow 'em up! Everyone go! We're taking that monster down with us!
Ugh... uaargh—!
He's... here.
The smoke clears, and a figure emerges from the end of the jet-black tunnel.
This monster brandishes no claws, has no twisted limbs, bears no sharp fangs nor keen incisors, nor grows any extraneous feelers.
He seems like nothing more than a young noble whose picture could be in the papers, one who'd speak frankly about Londinium's political and economical situation.
If only... the ground below his feet was not soaked in fresh blood.
What a terrible smell. Your men fight rather too dirty.
Monster...
Don't... Don't you think of getting near the commander...
M—My hand? Nothing even touched—agh!
Hah... now, this scent's much cozier.
I wouldn't mind... using the only thing of worth in your bodies to give the floor a wash.
The vampire airily lifts a finger, as if holding up a crystal chalice, or following the pulse of a piece of music.
The shadows leap up from beneath him, latching onto the soldiers' bodies, winding about their limbs.
What chilling screams follow are drowned entirely under the liquid that bursts forth.
The concentrated iron reek boring into nasal cavities. Bright red overflowing wherever one looks.
This is the instant the people stood at the tunnel's other end realize...
What chased them was no shadow at all. It was running blood, as thick as could be.
Is this... how Warfarin and Closure used to look?
That's the first thing that pops into your mind?!
Well, what the hell do you want me to think? It's better than being stark terrified, innit?
Run! Run like hell!
Where is the 'King of Sarkaz?'
What are you—
I come so far, go to all this trouble for an audience with her, and she cowers behind you frail little creatures, not even daring to meet me?
Agh—!
...Amiya.
He's here for Amiya.
You mean that King whatever he said? You're sure that's Amiya? Isn't Amiya a Cautus?
Forget it, it's not important.
But Amiya's not here...
That's good.
Um, fair. Even she might not cope best with this monster of a thing.
Wonder if her and the Doctor've almost won... *sigh* What am I thinking? Even if they can shut the city artillery down, we're about to be torn to scraps here.
Vina, what's our plan?
Run.
I don't want to... and I CAN'T die here.

lines.st: So run, Vina.
Run into the darkness before you. Don't think.
These tunnels will take you anywhere you wish to go.

lines.x: We can't keep this deadlock up anymore.
Doctor, step back.
I'm going to condense stronger energy. I have to defeat Manfred as quickly as possible.

opts: No.

lines.x: Doctor, please trust me. I've matured a lot since the last time I removed my inhibitors.
I knew I wouldn't let myself fall, just as we entered Londinium.

opts: He's hoping you'll do this.

lines.x: Ugh... his energy is densifying! It's like... like a net, from head, to toe... it's everywhere!
The air's... full of invisible Originium crystals! He's scattering them as beacons for his attacks!
No wonder! It's like invisible drones are throwing bombs all about!
Mr. Feist, you and Closure have to get away... if you get caught up, there'll be nowhere to hide!
You can use more strength than that, Cautus.
I'm forcing my Arts... against this energy flow...
Savor it. I formed this cage myself.
Am I less able to shake it... the more I struggle?
Cautus, you said my mind wasn't set on this battle with you. You were only half correct.
I'll deliver you bound before Her Majesty. The Sarkaz in Londinium... are loath to see an outblood flaunting her borrowed power from the King.
I can't, and I don't want to command them.
Of course you can't.
But they shouldn't be commanded by Theresis either.
When I stayed in Kazdel, I saw so many Sarkaz warriors. Plenty of them were mercenaries. After that, at Chernobog... I met them again.
Even when they were being exploited by Talulah, at least they knew who their enemy in each battle was, and knew they were giving up their lives for a future.
But now? You've trapped them inside a foreign city. Ever since the moment I came, I've sensed their frustration, their anxiety, their bewilderment...
You read my warriors' emotions?
The net's... tightening? Are you angry?
Cautus, has anyone ever told you you're too conceited? You're no more than an Infected. You've never experienced for yourself what truly constitutes a Sarkaz life.
You simply sit on the side and watch. What makes you think you can truly comprehend our pain, our resentment?!
It's true, I might not be able to comprehend.
But... the fire that blazes in your hearts, it's burned since the distant past into the present, from the first razing of Kazdel to today in Londinium, and even now, it's still within me.
It burns. It burns incomparably true, truer than any illusion that words or Arts could conjure.
Say your piece as you please, Infected.
I know how this power of yours originates.
Once a lie is spun, it will not be any less of a lie, no matter how convinced the liar is of it.
Sarkaz, Infected...
No matter how you identify each one of us, we're all just ordinary people wanting to live through the unjust circumstances we're put in.
But Theresis has brought everyone into a hopeless war—look at the Victorian dukes' armies outside the city.
All it takes is for them to point their lances the same way—it could be Kazdel, it could be Londinium, but all that awaits the Sarkaz then is ruin.
No matter what you're using to spur them on—bloodlines, authority, renown... it's those that are the total lies!
When did you reform your energy and make some scant space?
I was not wrong, Cautus.
—You are very dangerous.
Your power, your speech... It may be unable to shake our mightiest soldiers, but it certainly brings our enemies the delusion of hope. I don't want them struggling any further.
You have no choice but to stop here. I won't ever let you enter this city again—
But I will crush flat this cage of yours!
Cage? I'm not even sure it lives up to that.
Huh...?
Someone's altering the density of my Arts?
Wait, it's you again...
Doctor, am I a little late here?
Finding a route through Londinium's city walls, well... that's tough, even for me.
Lucky I found a few outstanding helpers.

opts: Appreciated. Thank you, Misery.
Amiya, we're switching battlefields.
Closure, position drones.
Feist, zipline.
And now, we jump.

lines.x: Minutes before...
Doctor, I've locked onto Manfred.
Uh, barely anybody's with him...

opts: He's not scared of us approaching him.

lines.x: You think he's overpowered? He looks like he eats fights for breakfast. Maybe Theresis taught him a bunch of moves.
Can we seriously pound him into stopping the artillery like a good boy with just five minutes?
No, scratch that, no I in this we. At this point, you can't count me and Feist as part of your combat force. Throwing Logistics guys into this fray'd be a hell of a way to waste lives.
Amiya, you're all we've got...
Manfred is a powerful enemy.
But we've faced powerful enemies to come all this way... and we'll surely face more still.

opts: Amiya, you keep all of Manfred's attention.
Closure, Feist, ready our retreat from the tower.
The one we're waiting for is almost here.

lines.x: Reprising old tricks? Did you really think I'd let you all escape a second time?
A thread of energy comes rushing in, as if it's locked onto some position in the air.
You almost feel the point of that sword slice at the tip of your feet.
Until a shield comes down in front of you all.
Drop any thoughts of pursuing them, Sarkaz.
Your fight is right here.
White Wolf. I didn't imagine you would come face me again.
So this passage is bidirectional, what a surprise. You sent them away, and brought yourself in at the same time?
It wasn't me... It was us.
Behind Horn, a soldier stands up.
And then a second, and then a third.
And now over a dozen Victorian soldiers block the Sarkaz general's way.
All of them, camouflages dropped.
They wear a dizzying array of different uniforms, the majority scarred in the roiling flames of war, some covered in mud and cobweb, some splattered by the blood of their own comrades.
Their weapons are just as motley, some still holding regulation swords, some instead toting self-fashioned crossbows.
But their faces all bear an identical vigor.
This time, they stand firm or fall once and for all. They will never retreat.
A gaggle of leftover forces thinks they can beat me?
You aren't fit to fight me, even with a weapon.
Have you forgotten you owe your last escape from my grasp entirely to that Rhodes Island Sarkaz meddler?
You can stop checking all around, Sarkaz.
When you don't respect your opponent on the battlefield, you're the first to fall.
Your blabbering may well be more stubborn than your shield.
Hrrgh––
Watch the enemy's attack angle! Assemble behind me!
I wasn't planning to eliminate you, but I didn't know you would be so eager to hold your own funeral.
Here I thought you'd have some level of tactical prowess, White Wolf.
You bring a platoon of wounded men to resist me, and have your soldiers give up their lives one after another? Is this your so-called professional spirit?
Don't... forget where you're stood right now.
This is the city wall of Londinium.
Don't go assuming you've already conquered her, Sarkaz.
—See for yourself.
We're still right here.
You're already down to your last few men.
On any past battlefield, Victoria would've needed at least two or more Steam Knights in order to hold me off.
But look at yourselves now—
You're just a bunch of plain soldiers.
—Hold formation!
Those who still have energy in your shields! Stand together with me!
Snipers, sustain fire, pin him!
Yes, Captain!
I've said it before—no matter how strong he is, he's still just a flesh-and-blood Sarkaz Infected. His Arts have a limit!
You want to wait until I cast my body dry?
You'll never live to see me exhaust my strength.
Ugh!
Blake!
Horn, take my sword—
—Got it.
When they fall, they turn their gazes to you, still with hope.
I never thought I'd see such brilliance in Londinium again.
But how much longer can this fragile light persist?
Horn!
...Robben?
*pant*... *pant*... No cause for alarm... falling here... just returning... returning... back to Londinium's land.
Sarkaz!!!
You fired all of your remaining ammunition at once?
Well done. You've stopped me in my tracks for the first time.
But after this, you're out of tricks.
After you watch your comrades die one by one, will you lose the will to fight too, White Wolf?

lines.st: Rita.
I can understand how disappointed you are in your father.
Yes, a soldier's blood flows through my body.
But just as Victoria invented steam trains, yet only decades later abandoned steam—
Modern Victoria has taken off the crown meant for people to lift their gazes at, and no longer needs soldiers like your great-grandmother or me to fight for her.
There'll come a day the White Wolf, together with the Lion Padishah he accompanied, will be no more than indistinct images left in the history books.
Your father's taken note of that. He's ground down his own sharp claws so the family isn't torn apart by the tides of the ages.
But you're different.
You grow in a changing Victoria.
Rita Skamandros, you won't need to fight a landtrotter's campaign in the name of the Aslan King.
But Victoria is still your home.

lines.x: Sarkaz... So long as you occupy our cities, I will always have a reason to fight.
My desire to defeat you is shared with every Victorian.
Your shield is of little use now.
I still have a sword.
Your sword... heh, common as could be.
You want to battle me with a sword like this?
*huff*... Don't underestimate it, or me.
You're so fond of remembering... the Steam Knights...
When you take off the armor that Victoria forged them...
They turn out just to be—
Victorians!
We actually... got inside? Why has this wall turned so soft?
Not bad! For an instant back there, I thought I was gonna get my head painted over Londinium's walls!
It was Misery's Arts. He wouldn't see us hurt.
Right... Kal'tsit told me your brain was an important asset to Rhodes Island.
My hands don't come cheap either, c'mon!
After that, I'm never asking to hitchhike with you again... Blaze carrying me everywhere is a way smoother ride than your Arts!
Am I right or am I right, Doctor?

opts: Your hands are always busy. That's gotta be something.
I wouldn't say the Blaze Express is THAT comfortable.
You'd better take a breather, Misery.

lines.x: I agree. Misery, I saw it clearly... Manfred almost impaled you with that thrust.
That was just a desperation blow. He had no other move.
You successfully provoked his rage, and that made him overlook my approach. I didn't even think I could fool him twice.
I didn't at all intend to...
When he asked me that, I definitely felt his anger. That rage of his rushed at me like a heatwave.
If Manfred uses all his strength, anyone nearby would have trouble lasting for long. Best I... *cough* *cough* *cough*...
Misery, I know you want to help that Victorian officer, but using large-scale matter-transformation Arts several times in one day is too taxing on your body.
It's okay, Amiya.
I won't drop dead for a while yet. Besides, I promised Kal'tsit...
You elite operators... make plenty of promises to Dr. Kal'tsit, don't you?

Inject these emergency meds into yourself. The fight in Londinium's only just begun, and I don't want to see you off somewhere alone again, swallowing down all the blood you're practically throwing up.
Amiya, enemies inbound.

opts: Amiya, cut them off!
Feist, tie up six ziplines to me.
Closure, let's plant the bombs.

lines.x: Hm. Misery found us a weak point in Londinium's defense system... We need to see this control room destroyed.
Blistering speed—White Wolf, this was your true aim, wasn't it?
Fangs and claws as fierce as these, yet tamed by Victoria's noble etiquette... Was it really worth it?
Sarkaz, wasn't your present appearance once packaged in so-called culture?
I hear your people used to look very different.
You can try your hardest, but you still cannot kill me. So long as this city's defenses continue to churn—
No. Wait. White Wolf, you're only stalling me!
You—You and Rhodes Island are collaborating again.
Do you know who they are? Do you know what that Cautus leader...?
I only know—that they can stop you.
White Wolf, should you miraculously succeed today... you will witness a corner of Londinium's walls collapse, with your own eyes.
Is that what you want to see? From this moment on, the legend of Londinium everstanding may well no longer—
From this moment on?
The fact you can be stood here means... the idea that Londinium has never fallen is nothing more than a self-reassuring joke.
Damn you! If those dukes outside take note of this—
Are you aware? You're finally showing a hint of panic in your face.
That goes to show my decision was the correct one.
If this wall... only exists to smother the screams of the Victorians from within, then it's already lost its founding purpose.
You ought to let those dukes hear!
Hear... how our fellow citizens cry out under your slaughter!
How... How much longer until we can go outside?
Oh... it feels like this passage is about to collapse... are we all going to die here?
Don't look down, and don't look back.
If the fear gets too much, just stare at the way ahead.
The signal light's still on. This is our hope that our soldiers lit for us.
We wait for the light as it turns bright... and then we can catch sight of the sun in Londinium once again.
Their firepower's too strong—
Rockrock, we can't get any closer to the train.
The bombardment hasn't stopped... Can the people down below still hold out? How much time do we have left?
There's been no word from the commander's end...
I... I believe in my family.
That's the one and only reason I've kept on this far.
Bloody hell, I can't get these things off me!
That's not it... You saw how fast they were a moment ago?
He's slowing them on purpose, like he's... just watching how long we can run for...
What kind of sicko is he?
Mmm... listen to the sound of your breaths, your pulses. Are you finally tired out?
Is this... the honor of the Sarkaz?
This isn't fit to be called a battle.
...Battle?
You seem to have the wrong idea.
The 'King of Sarkaz' isn't here, so I'm left with no matching opponent at all.

Would you converse with a meatbeast about glory or honor? When you know full well that hunting them serves to do nothing but excite your senses, that their only ultimate service is as an entrée on your dinner table?
You treat us... as prey?
You may be a Sarkaz, but slaughtering like this out of pure personal taste is still far too barbaric.
Do not appraise me by your understanding of culture. It would be the most hideous kind of slander to me.
Try and resist me, before the filthy rubble pummels you into mincemeat here.
I will borrow your blood to record your feelings at death's door... all your fear, unwillingness, anger... Let them burst forth upon me to your heart's content!
Vina, get over here—
No.
Vina!
Morgan, you fall back too.
Urgh...
You're going to stand in front of all of us, and resist him?
Yes.
You're no match for him.
I know.
And you're not planning to turn and flee, even if you'll be shredded in an instant, have your flesh leave the dullest stain in this whole tunnel?
Only if... you can all run faster than I can.
Why?
Because...
There is no because. I just want to do this.
When the city above us falls towards us, I want to be the first to embrace it in that instant.
If that's so...
The signal light in the passage seems to glow brighter.
Are her eyes failing her? Why is there starlight in Londinium's underground? No... how long have they gone without seeing the stars behind the clouds, even on the surface?
Siege blinks.
She suddenly realizes that is no star.
It is a button.
The detritus that caked it has been shaken off, and now it is shining, and she has the exact fortune to know what it does.
She was first brought underneath the palace at three years of age.
Her mentor carried her upon his back, using a tremendous paw to guide her in pushing this button.
Londinium's subterranean sprawled about its own muscle and bone, exposing to the future liege its veins and arteries, filled with life.
Over twenty years later—
She has found it for the second time.
Bombs mounted at all nodes!
Doctor, I've got our retreat ready!
Move it, they're in here, stop them—
Urgh...
Amiya! Dammit, how are they coming so thick and fast... did Misery leave too late? Did they not stall Manfred?
I'll hold them off! All of you, go!
Doctor, give the detonator to me—

opts: No.

lines.x: You won't have a chance to retreat unless I lock down these enemies!
Once you've successfully withdrawn, I'll blow up this control room straight away—

opts: No.

lines.x: Doctor, we can't wait any longer! Think of the Self-Salvation Corps' fighters, Victoria's soldiers, Lady Heidi and Siege's team...
And every single Victorian being bombarded right now! None of us can hold on anymore!

opts: Amiya, take my hand.
Tell me—when to push the detonator.

lines.x: Doctor...!
*cough* *cough*... These black lines are everywhere... I can't see anything!
Now!
Doctor, why are you... in front of me?

lines.st: Because I want to be.

lines.x: I'm not... not the weak child I was anymore...
I won't drag you down anymore. I can withstand these Arts, and these flames.

lines.st: I know.
You're so powerful now. Far more powerful than I am, probably.
But no matter how many opportunities you give me to choose...
No matter if we're confronted with a Draco's flames, or the fiery light of these explosions...
This is what I want to do.

lines.x: Doctor...
Oof... caught you!
Wondered why you asked me for those six ziplines, Doctor. This is what it was for?
It'll be hard to move about, but... oh well, not as if you run fast ordinarily...
Fly, my drones!
How's that for a surprise, Doctor? In response to your demands, I've tinkered on my drones and finally developed—a people-towing glider function!
There's a cap on its long-term use, but at least it'll get us safely on the ground—

opts: Now's the time to dial up the explosions.

lines.x: The control room—!
They... did it...
Urgh...
How... How long ago did you tire out? You can't even brandish your sword now...
You really were doing nothing but... making a stand with all you had.
Anyone else with your wounds would be in a coma ten times over by now!
Now you realize? Slow, aren't you.
General, the explosion in the control room has affected the cannons. The batteries and the minor section of the wall structure here have all been severely damaged—
If we hang around here any longer, we'll fall!
Notify all hands, retreat!
What about this soldier?
I've defeated her already, but toppling her would require spending more effort still.
And it's not worth spending the time here any longer.
Ha... haha...
White Wolf of Victoria, you are a worthy opponent.
Should you live to descend this city wall—
Then next time we meet, by my sword... I will send you to a death befittingly heroic.
Next time...
Will there be a next time?
Londinium's wall is falling down.
And so she endlessly falls too.
I... I did it.
Until... you all left...
...I did not fall.
Her blood flows out, and she almost loses the last speck of awareness she has.
But her body is as if a part of her sword and shield at this point, harder than steel, unbending unless shattered.
