The weapon forged by Victoria still stands upon Londinium's wall.
So the soldier She tempered to fruition keeps her watch upon it too.
Until a corner of it crashes to its city's ground—
And she returns to the land that raised her.
Just how tall... is this wall?
Why is it like...
...I'm flying up?
Horn, can you still move? Grab my hand, quick!
...Robben?
You're... not...
Yes, I'm not dead! I was lucky enough to grab onto the outside structure when I fell, and then Mr. Misery here saved me again!
Come, soldier. You haven't finished your fight yet.
You lot, honestly. You won't ever let me rest, will you?
Nope!
Horn, don't shut your eyes! Don't forget, your soldier is still here! You can't fall!
Robben, you've turned as noisy as... as a certain someone, now.
Oh, forget it... I've never been able to do anything about you lot.
Her vision becomes clear once again.
The city wall of Londinium is still there, and though its highest height is now adorned by the tiniest hole, it still seems as towering, as unyielding as ever.
What's that shaking?
Artillery finally do the underground structure in?
No, that's not it.
This shaking's coming from underneath us—
Vina, what did you do?
I've found a path we can walk.
Is this whole tunnel... moving up?
How? You'd think we'd know it could do that after living here for so long, right?
There are always some 'locals' who'll know more than others.
That monster...
Is it still gonna come after?
It will.
But maybe not today.
So you found a new course to flee down?
Ah... but of course, but of course.
This scent... is it that Feline, giving it off?
No... so-called royal families are no more than a lie, so called to consolidate power and coin, as laughable as this nation's culture.
Even if she is that runaway's cub, the smell of her blood would be no different to any other Feline.
So, this completely different scent...
Interesting.
Does Theresis know about this?
You'll have no chance to verify for yourself.
Oh?
I remember who you are. You're that... little disciple by Theresis's side. Even if your teacher was stood right here, he would be troubled to apprehend me.
He's no longer fit to be my teacher anymore.
Hm?
This odor of cursecraft... So you've come too, Banshee? I'd thought you were in that city right now, playing games with my entourage.
Trifling, unpracticed Artists. They weren't enough to hinder my step.
*sigh*... That makes things tricky.
This underpass on the verge of collapse is no fine place for the Royal Court to congregate.
Am I nothing in his eyes? Just because I didn't learn my skills from Theresis, or have any noble blood?
This long-winded old man's genuinely pissed me off. I'm going to gift him a hundred bombs as a farewell present. Any of you have any objections?
...Report.
Rhodes Island's people have jumped out from the control room. They had that Cautus, so we couldn't snipe them—
And the rebels in the underground tunnels?
Er, we detected an explosion from beneath... and the Sanguinarch hasn't returned yet.
Where is Hoederer?
He's severely wounded, and currently unconscious.
Assemble what workers remain, and have them inspect the city defense systems and report the damages. We must be prepared to activate more batteries... and even the main cannons.
Surround the remaining individuals at the station. The surviving rebels will be sure to pass through there.
The... The scouts are reporting, they've detected another group rapidly closing in on the station—
Manfred, don't blame yourself too much. You've already tried your hardest.
What are you doing here—
Rockrock, take a look at the city wall—
The defense artillery's been... shut down?
Send the signal!
They're coming, they're all coming!
But the Sarkaz... they're all rushing this way!
Stop them! Kill every last one!
If we can just repel the Sarkaz, we can start up the train!
You're not getting away that easily!
Ugh—
How's the rebel army coming from that way too?
Is that reinforcements?
A team's broken through the Sarkaz forces' blockade from the north side!
Who... Who called them in?
Did some of the Messengers we sent to the city center survive? Did they get to ask for help?
You've done a fine job, my Sudean friends!
You can leave the next battle to us now! We're more on the professional end!
The way they work... they must have loads of experience...
Is... Is that Rhodes Island's operator?
Break them up!
Make a passage, ensure the Self-Salvation fighters withdraw from the underpass safely!
Snipers, take down those signalers—
It's—Miss Montague!
Montague?
That name rings a bell and a half! Some noble in the paper or something?
Don't get distracted!
No matter who they are, we're on the same side!
We can get through! Quick, lead everyone onto the train!
Oh, wow... Lady Heidi, we've made it...
Mm-hm, I told you. Our soldiers won't let us down.
They're her people.
Did our Messengers get in contact with her?
To be more precise, it was your Rhodes Island Messenger.
Dagda! Look, it's Dagda!
That tart...
Hm.
There among the crowd, Dagda sees them too.
She throws down the Sarkaz she's tussling with, and rushes through the passage made with no hesitation, arms reached out to her partners caked in grime.
I... I was so scared I'd be late.
No, you weren't.
No matter when you come back, we could never call you late.
Bastard, you... you stormed off just like that, remember? I didn't even get to beat you up!
Hm... you're still in time to do it now.
Ay, Vina, did you know she'd come back?
I knew.
I get you hid it from Indra. She'd never take help from nobles' mercenaries unless it was critical as this, end-of-the-line type.
Stop griping about me and let's get these idiots dealt with first!
I never stopped here!
Vina, I didn't finish... you could've at least given me a shout!
*sigh* You had me wondering if Dagda was off in some corner again even while we were being killed! I was all ready to take the Sarkaz down with me!
I'm sorry, Morgan...
This won't happen again. I swear.
Siege Group, fully assembled.
We just wait for the Doctor's lot to come back, and we can all get out of here together.
Everyone, we're back!
Amiya?
Fancy coming in from the sky, all of you...
We're touching down! Gangway, outta the way—
G—Grab the zipline! Don't crash into the train, it's our only means of escaping alive!
I've got the Doctor held, we're alright!
Safe landing!
We... We actually made it?
Rockrock—
Commander, is Rockrock alright? Everyone evacuated out?
We have more survivors than anticipated.
It's thanks to you, Feist, and everyone you brought in from Rhodes Island.
Hah... You see that, Old Bill?
No, don't you faint on the job now! The battle's still not done!
If you can move, then come help me in the engine room!
There are still Sarkaz forces pursuing us.
There's nothing they can do, now.
Because we'll hold them off here.
Right, damned right.

opts: Us, together.

lines.x: Commander, the train's setting off—
All ready?
Amiya, we're getting on!
Alright!
Go!

lines.st: Amiya?
Amiya, careful. The train's moving.
Amiya... what are you looking at?

lines.x: The Cautus girl does not reply to you. Her body is still. Even her breathing seems to have stopped.
You can tell she's shocked. She's never let emotions as unveiled and intense show before the operators. This moment is incomparable.
At the same time, the Sarkaz chasing the train all come to a halt.
They all turn their heads, looking in the same direction.
A Sarkaz enters the ruins.
All smoke seems to part at her feet, all sounds of fire suspended about her.
Only wind remains, growing stronger and fiercer.
The ash laden on the ground is blown about, some sticking to her attire, some smudging her pale hair. She seems not to mind at all, only silently gazing into a point of the ruins.
It seems to have once been part of the defense artillery. A jet-black fowlbeast is there now, perched atop the still-warm cannon mouth.
Most likely disturbed by the train picking up in speed, it shakes off its wings.
As if somewhat in response, the pale-haired Sarkaz turns around.
She should have no sight of who is aboard the train, but her expression is as if she has recognized who is looking at her.
Her face is so warm, yet her eyes are so filled with sorrow. The sorrow is so much; perhaps not all of it comes from her, but comes from the one who stares at her too.

lines.st: Amiya, are you alright?

lines.x: The Cautus girl finally comes back to reality.
She still doesn't give you a response, and you're in no rush for her to give one.
In one ear, you hear the calls of fowlbeasts. You see a flock of them out of the window flapping through the air, rushing into the gale ahead of you.
The train takes you rapidly from the station.
The city wall, the war, and that pale-haired figure, all are left far, far behind you.
Rumble. Rumble. Rumble.
Is that the remnants of cannon fire lingering in your brain, the train bumping against the railway, or thunder in those gloomy clouds gathering not far away?
The center of Londinium is right before you.
And all of you are riding into this storm.
Your Majesty.
Regretfully... I was late.
Does... does Your Majesty share the same regrets I do? That we were not able to stop them here?
Manfred...
Back then... in Kazdel, you would never speak to me so guardedly.
Back then, you'd look me straight in the eye.
Your Majesty, Kazdel was nothing more than a cluttered ruin and a thick stack of blueprints. In all those years, you and the general...
No, as things are now, there is no need to speak of past matters.
Your Majesty has returned to the general's side. Together, you shall rule the Royal Court and bring the Sarkaz victory once again, just as countless times in the past.
For me and the rest of the men, we need only remember this.
Yes...
Even so, Manfred... I really wish to meet the girl.
Your Majesty!
Do you think a person dreams, in death?
I've dreamt of them often, the past few months.
Again and again I dream of that ship. I dream of conversations with that doctor of few smiles. I dream of standing shoulder to shoulder with that silent commander on the ship's deck. I dream of...
...I dream of sitting by her bedside every night, telling tales of the Sarkaz and this great land, lulling the girl to sleep.
Is... the general aware?
He knows all.
However, a dream is but a dream...Isn't that so?
Your Majesty, your cheeks...
Ah... It's just ash.
I thought...
I do not shed tears.
Yes... Why do I not shed tears?
Your Majesty, in the end you still allowed 'her' to go to the city walls?
You are aware of the threat the 'King of Sarkaz' bears.
Of course. Not even Manfred or the Sanguinarch may be capable of successfully capturing the successor to that power.
——To defeat a 'Sarkaz King', the safest choice is naturally another one.
Did you not intend that when you raised that suggestion to me?
I also remember that you nearly killed me when I first suggested to bring 'her' back.
'Vomit inducing'—— That's what you said. It's difficult to forget such fury.
My opinion has not changed.
But we still need 'her', do we not?
Only when the two of you stand together, will the soldiers truly believe that the chaotic and dark era of the past is now history.
They will never again question the legitimacy of your orders. There will no longer be two different voices in the Sarkaz's hearts.
To this end, we must get hold of the other 'Sarkaz King' as soon as possible.
Then go and make sure of that.
The soldiers must focus on the war ahead.
They must clearly understand that they are following a ruler who can lead them out of their plight, and not just some power of dubious ownership.
I know, I know. You have never cared for that black crown.
For your satisfaction, I have never stopped researching the 'Sarkaz King' either.
Thanks to 'her', I've made great progress. Should we get our hands on that Cautus too... Perhaps, we will be able to truly understand the power of the 'Sarkaz King' before too long.
The Sarkaz are not slaves to any man or power.
If the crown of the 'Sarkaz King' is but another shackle...
Then, crush it.
Your wish aligns with my own... It will be done, Your Majesty.
I did not expect you to return so late, Your Highness.
...Keh.
You seem... tired.
Confessarius, you neglected to inform me that the banshee was there too.
Ah... I thought you'd have guessed it.
Hmph.
A banshee can be more trouble to deal with than a nachzehrer. What frightening talent.
That said, next time I'll simply stick his tongue in a crystal case and gift it to his mother.
The Regent and the Nachzehrer King will not be pleased, Your Highness.
Then they'd best not find out.
Also, that Manfred... How dare he use those toys mounted on the city walls on me?
Half the filth on me is thanks to him. Perhaps I should teach him a lesson.
I understand your frustrations, Your Highness.
Consider this, as a tiny compensation for the missing intel, I will suggest the Regent grant you the task of capturing the 'Sarkaz King'.
Heh, that bastard failed after all. I've been telling Theresis all this time he's just a soft-hearted coward.
It's fine, all of this is within the Regent's plans.
Let them celebrate for now. After all, the best way to crush hope is to first let it come to fruition.
Hah... The nobles in the city grow restless once more. You're right, let them continue with their folly.
When the banquet is at its peak, the abrupt shifting of laughter to horror... it's tantalizing.
Incidentally, speaking of the nobles——
Is there anything Your Highness wishes to report to the Regent?
No, nothing.
Tell Theresis... I await the next banquet.
He's obviously discovered something, but he's hiding it?
These little moves... I fear the Regent will not tolerate him much longer.
Leader.
Any movement from the Duke's forces outside the city?
The Nachzehrer King will be returning soon. Once he arrives, send him to meet the Regent immediately.
Also... didn't you mention a Victorian noble sent some Messengers back?
Let us go and meet them.
Sir, there's one more thing.
Is there?
She... actually chose to return, at such a time.
Yes...
Do you have a plan, sir?
Send a message to the Royal Academy of Sciences.
It's time... for a long awaited family reunion.
Liz... How are you feeling? Are we going too fast? Are you tired?
...I'm fine.
You have to tell me if you feel any discomfort.
Yes... my chest feels a bit stuffy.
Is it because you're getting closer to home?
No, that isn't my home.
Shining... My home is with you and Nearl.
I didn't want you to come back here.
But I wanted to.
I want to go with you, and... I want to find what I've lost.
I... I can feel that they're here.
Yes, Nightingale. We'll find them, and they'll help you get better. You are going to get better.
And then... we'll return to Rhodes Island. Or we could also go to Kazimierz... We'll go wherever you want.
You'll... go with me, right?
Of course, Liz.
No matter when, as long as you want it... I will stay by your side.

lines.st: It feels like going on a brand new journey, while also returning to the starting point.
Its high walls give Londinium the feeling of a giant cage, or even a sealed graveyard. But it is also the past and present home of countless people.
This place hides many answers, yet it also leads to a great deal more questions.
Where are we going?
To a reunion... or a separation?

lines.x: Are we... alive?
Yes, it looks like we can finally take a breather.
*Sniffles*... Waaah!
Rockrock, w-why are you crying? I-I-I've never seen you cry before!
It's nothing, I just... I thought of Bill. When I think of him, I just...
Old Bill... *sigh*.
*Sniffles*...*cough* N-No. I shouldn't do this.
The battle... isn't over.
It's fine, Rockrock. We're safe right now. We can take a break, and think of Old Bill and our other friends...
Before the next battle starts. It's what we ought to do.
We meet at last.
You are...?
Allerdale Cumberland. Call me Allerdale.
I'm Clovisia's friend, and the Londinium Citizens' Self-Salvation Corps' officer-in-charge of Auchterigg borough——that is, Central Londinium.
Cumberland?
You're the Duke of Cumberland's...
...Daughter.
I was just a small child when Father passed away.
I only remember him brushing his sweat-laden beard on my face whenever he returned from a fencing match——This is the only memory I have left after twenty years.
I heard that the Duke of Cumberland... was betrayed by other anti-royalist dukes.
Father walked the path he believed in, for the sake of his ideals.
I respect him greatly, which is why I'm here.
You and the other nobles within the city... You've been fighting against the Sarkaz?
Yes, but our efforts have only just begun to bear fruit.
I was extremely surprised when Isabelle came to look for me.
Not just because she's the Count of Manchester's successor——Although the count has always been one of our most steadfast supporters.
It's just that I thought all the Knights of the Tower were dead...Isabelle's return was a great inspiration to many of us.
Of course, what surprised me the most was... you.
You know who I am?
Haha, I was not aware at first. How could I be 'surprised' otherwise?
All these years, we have never stopped looking for you, but there were never any clues. Many thought that you were long dead...
I never believed so.
I do believe many of those looking for me actually wanted to take my life.
Perhaps so...
But even among those, there are many who've changed their minds.
You've seen what's happened to Londinium——
People lost their freedom long ago.
All Victorians, even those nobles holding tightly onto their titles, must either become a slave to the Sarkaz, or tremble in fear as they hope to survive till the next day.
Even deep in slumber, they instinctively cry out 'Your Majesty'——
Is there anyone who has not wondered, should that sudden execution all those years ago have gone another way, would Londinium be as it is today?
Do you really think that my presence here would change anything?
At the very least, it opens up a new possibility.
Even though you appear to be contemplating, I know you have already set your heart on it.
You sent Isabelle to contact me. Is that not a sign that you're prepared to lead us against the Sarkaz?
I don't deny that I've made a decision.
But being prepared... you can never be fully prepared.
You're not wrong.
We should begin, Lady Cumberland... Allerdale. Please walk me through our current situation again.
I'm sure you understand that I have a lot to catch up on.
Great, I love your enthusiasm. That's just what we need right now.
Then...
Welcome home, Alexandrina... Your Majesty.
Heidi.
Kal'tsit... *Huff*, Kal'tsit.
——It's tough to get a meeting with you.
Did anyone follow you?
Don't worry, I've carefully covered my tracks all the way here.
How could I be your Messenger if I wasn't even capable of that?
You've already done very well. Your father would be proud.
Haha...
Frankly, many times in the past when I was close to failure... I would think of you, and wonder what you would do.
I would also think about what you would say, if I could get everyone to make it through with me...
But, now that you're really standing before me, I suddenly feel that all those things I've imagined mean nothing.
Heidi, you are well prepared.
Indeed.
At least... I will always be prepared. All of us will.
By the way, are you in a hurry to see Amiya?
I've confirmed that she is currently safe.
It's been a long time since I was in Londinium, so I have a number of matters I need to take care of.
That's good.
There's someone... She's been waiting to meet you.
Kal'tsit, start talking. I need an explanation.
——You're alive.
And what exactly do you wish me to explain, W?
You're as calm as ever. Hard for me to tell if you knew before you got here or not.
Whatever, it doesn't even matter if you're a monster or not. I've got bigger fins to fry.
It's Theresa... it really was Theresa!
You——How could you let Theresis get his hands on her body?!

opts: Amiya.

lines.x: Ah... Sorry, Doctor. I didn't see you coming.

opts: You've been preoccupied ever since the train.
What did you see on the train?

lines.x: Yes...
I guess there's no hiding it... And I shouldn't hide it from you anyway.
Dr. Doctor, I saw...her.
Doctor... Miss Rockrock really was talking about her.

opts: Kal'tsit said she already...
Kal'tsit said I...
And W's attitude towards me...

lines.x: Four years ago, Theresa passed away.
Even though Dr. Kal'tsit and W may disagree with me about the truth behind this... There's one thing we definitely wouldn't get wrong.
That day, we really lost her.

opts: What's this about...?

lines.x: ...I don’t know.
But... the moment I saw her at the train station, I finally understood... why I felt those emotions in Londinium.
Besides anger... sorrow. An endlessly infinite sorrow.
Yes... to the Sarkaz, anger and sorrow are symbiotic. It's just that they choose to dry their tears with anger most of the time.
And all of those emotions seemed to converge in her eyes.
Even Theresa had never...

opts: You're hurting.

lines.x: Hurting? Maybe, but compared to them... those Sarkaz shattered by hate and prejudice, this is nothing.
I can feel it... It's too little, far too little...
I glimpsed it for a moment, but I was nearly drowned under that turbulent wave of emotion.
What did Theresa go through?

opts: That's not your responsibility.

lines.x: Is it really not?
Doctor, I said before that I really couldn't believe it was her.
But... when I saw her with my own two eyes, I found it wasn't very difficult at all to accept.
Since I'm here, I need to know the truth.
Not just the truth behind Theresa, but also the Sarkaz within the city... What exactly did they experience, that they would be burdened with such anger and sorrow?

opts: And if the truth brings about more pain?

lines.x: Dr. Doctor...

opts: You can be afraid, Amiya.
I'm afraid too, Amiya.
But we will walk onwards together.

lines.x: Yeah!
Dr. Doctor, together...
Let's look for the answer.

opts: This is also an answer I need.

lines.x: I apologize for interrupting you——
There's an urgent matter concerning Alexandrina... I mean Vina, as well as our Self-Salvation Corps.
Based on a letter from Lady Heidi, we've confirmed that this matter relates to you.
Dr. Doctor——
Is Edward Artorius's relic in your possession?

lines.st: '...Next, turn your textbooks to page 132.
Terran Calendar 31, the Empire of Hippogryph was officially renamed the Empire of Ursus. We have already spent two lectures talking about and analyzing this piece of history.

Historians believe that the cause of the Hippogryphs' fall from power was primarily its ruling class's corruption and incompetence, as well as the rise of Ursus and the forces of production that it represents.

This war is often referred to as Ursus's uprising, representing the Ancients' standing up against the Elders' authority. From this point onward, the legitimacy of the Elders' rulership across Terra gradually waned.
Yet if we look back on this war from a different perspective, we'll find a set of questions that naturally follow.
Suppose it was another Ursus race that had been ruling the country instead of the Hippogryphs. Would the revolution still have taken place?
If, as the rulers, the Hippogryphs had not insisted on a policy of racial discrimination, if they stopped taking Ursus wealth as their own and gave the Ursus equal opportunity...
Would the Hippogryph Empire's downfall have been averted, or would it have come sooner?
What if... the Khagan's cavalry never set foot on these lands?'

lines.x: Hello, are you here to listen in on Miss Koshelna's lecture?
Miss... Koshelna?
That's right. Her lectures on imperial history are very popular.
I've been noticing young men and women such as yourself listening in from the hallway. You aren't one of our students, are you? You don't look like a college student.
You can actually go inside if you'd like. She's not going to mind if you stand in the back of the classroom. You'll be able to hear her more clearly.
No need. Her voice is pretty clear as is.
Okay, then. This lecture is almost over anyway. I still have other lectures to attend, so please excuse me.
By all means, please.

lines.st: 'You may not be able to find the answers to these questions in any textbooks.
But I hope you will give them some thought. Let them occupy your brain as you eat, as you bathe, and even as you sleep...
And when an idea has lingered in your brain long enough, write it down. I'm very interested in your opinions.
Okay, let's wrap it up here. Enjoy your brief freedom until the next bell.'

lines.x: Do you have any questions, Miss?
Found you.
Why did she go into the city?
To look for someone.
And why is this someone worth the risk?
Nine, you said it yourself. We can't get anywhere close to the nomadic cities while we're in Ursus.
We want to pass judgment on her, don't we? Before she talks, she needs an answer still.
Without this answer, she won't even believe half a word she says herself, and neither will we.
We shouldn't be believing her to begin with!
She went into the city herself. What if she makes a run for it? All the efforts and sacrifices that went into rescuing her and all our plans about her going forward will all go to waste!
They won't.
She knows very well that she has nowhere to go.
Besides, as long as you still trust me, I can guarantee you this. No matter where she goes, I will take her in again.
I trust you... but that's based on everything you've done for us in the past year.
You're not like her. You don't talk big. This is something that all of us like about you. It's a good thing, and you should make sure you don't start picking up that habit.
Are you worried I'll be influenced by her?
I don't think that'll happen so easily.
Nine, a patrol unit is approaching—
Stay hidden.
Just as we predicted. In half an hour, this route won't be so safe anymore.
She better come back in time.
I thought I would've seen you much sooner—
—Talulah.
Koshelna... Couldn't have picked a more obvious name, could you, Kashchey?
Ah... Names. Names are just a way to refer to people. If the sound of this name reminds you of certain unpleasant memories, you can call me Fiolet.
Never mind. Your name is of little importance to me. Just like your appearance, your name is not worth remembering.
Why have you come then, Talulah?
Because you want a definite answer from me? Because you want me to tell you that, yes, Kashchey has left your body, and you're just Talulah and no one else?
In that case, you will get what you want. I can tell you exactly what you want to hear right now.
The question then is... are you willing to believe me?
You want me to believe a Black Snake?
Is this a new trick you're playing, Kashchey?
You gave me more than enough information to find you in the shortest amount of time. I can only assume that you were luring me here.
Have you gone back to our home, then? Our home, both yours and mine?
That's not my home. It's nothing more than ground zero of a conspiracy, a nest beloved of venomous snakes.
That's an awfully hurtful way to describe it.

But you read my letter nonetheless, didn't you? You remembered the channels through which Duke Kashchey received the Scales' correspondence. Not even the Secret Police who took over the dukedom could figure it out.
A letter sent ten years ago, and its sender is tenured here at this college.

I don't know if I should be gasping in admiration of how well prepared you are, or if I should be grateful that this channel stayed hidden for ten years while your Scales have long been reduced to nothing?
Does it make you happy that the territory has fallen into such neglect? Talulah, under your rule, they could have lived a much better life. They shouldn't have been torn to shreds by the Fourth Army.
Stop pretending you care about them more than I do.
Tell me. What is there in this small northern town for you to willingly stay here?
Kashchey, if I remember correctly, you preferred cities further south.
Not only because the air there is more humid, but also because lands where you cultivated generations of authority are more suitable for your scheming.
But not the tundra. The folk here lead a hard life. They have plenty to worry about sorting out issues with their food and harvest.
To them, the speeches you pride yourself on are like the artificial flower you wear. No purpose beyond aesthetics.
Talulah... Talulah.
What if I told you I'm here because your footsteps can be found in the tundra?
I'd say you're feeding me nonsense.
'The history of humanity is a history of struggle.'
You remember, don't you?
You applied everything that I taught you through the years to your struggle.
If you remember all of that, then shouldn't Talulah, the Fighter of the Tundra, and Talulah, the woman who succeeded the title of duke, be one and the same to me?
Yet you destroyed everything that I built in the tundra.
Did I?
Who do you truly blame for this series of fatal mistakes in your heart?
If you actually thought yourself innocent, you wouldn't have needed a year and a half to ponder how you should die.
The very moment your head turns, you will throw yourself into the next struggle.
Talulah, I know why you came to me.
You want to use your judgment on me to judge yourself. You want to hear me repent, so that you can start repenting yourself.
What you want is a tiny, pitiable bit of mental comfort.
Go on.
You want me to go on? I thought—
You thought I would let you witness my fury? No, Kashchey. You don't deserve it.

I've rehearsed everything you've said up to this very moment in my head, down to the very last word. A year and a half isn't that short, and I had nothing to do while I was imprisoned at Rhodes Island.
If you want to survive this meeting, you'll need to say something more original.
Very well. I can tell you've gotten more patient, thanks to that period of self-imprisonment.
But I brought up the tundra not because I wanted to provoke you.
Take a look at this school. Look at the students' faces.
They believe that the sunlight of may will melt the tundra's snow. They also believe that Ursus, colder and harder than even the tundra, will change beneath their feet.
So you're sullying their innocence with your despicable ideas?
You think I'm trying to find the next successor among them?
No, my dear. I'm just a normal university lecturer. The only things that I impart here are knowledge and ideas. I hold no authority or wealth.
If they want authority, they can fight for it themselves. If they seek a conspiracy, they are free to find the most suitable techniques in my lessons.
You want me to believe that the failure you experienced through me devastated you so much that you washed your hands of it all?
Failure? I never thought of that as failure.
Reunion was not destroyed in the Chernobog core city, and neither was Kashchey's plan.
Right, you managed to stop Kashchey. The war he sought, the war that could've changed Ursus history in an instant never came to be.
But Ursus still went on the trajectory that Kashchey had set for her.
War is happening. The conflict between the Council and the Army intensified, and the will of the people is sparking new flames through each and every conflict.
And I will stand atop the most fertile soil, sowing seeds to the best of my abilities.
I will guide... or perhaps I should say spur these revolutions to take root.
I remember you once refuted the education of the masses as meaningless.
If a ruler is so inflexible that she does not adapt to the changing tides, then she is undoubtedly following a path of destruction.
You still see yourself as a ruler. I see. You actually haven't changed at all.
I almost forgot you hated that word as much as you hated Kashchey's control. I told you long ago that I didn't care who was in charge.
How many times has power changed hands since the Ursus took these lands from the Hippogryphs?
Talulah, you are me, and I am you.
What we want... is an Ursus that can yet spark fires from our hands.
These fires... will sooner or later engulf all of Ursus. No, not just Ursus. Victoria... and all the other countries will be caught in the flames too.
When the fire is over, will these lands become more prosperous? Or will they be dealt destruction?
Ursus has walked its first millennium. I will guide it down its second and third. This is the meaning behind the Black Snake's existence.
The Black Snake is immortal not because I want to live, but because Ursus must live.
The Aslan heir has returned to Londinium?
It seems that way.
The Sarkaz gave us this information in exchange for the Sarkaz Messenger we returned to them.
'In exchange?'
They're merely using us to escalate the scuffle among the dukes.
It couldn't be more obvious, but we can also tell from their methods that the situation in Londinium has changed, and the Sarkaz are anxious.
